description: Gather relevant information from multiple print and digital sources, using search terms effectively; assess the credibility and accuracy of each source; and quote or paraphrase the data and conclusions of others while avoiding plagiarism and following a standard format for citation.
description_plain: Your child finds useful information from books and the internet, checks if the sources are trustworthy, and shares the information correctly without copying.

subject: Language Arts
description: Conduct short research projects to answer a question (including a self-generated question), drawing on several sources and generating additional related, focused questions that allow for multiple avenues of exploration.
description_plain: Your child does short research projects to answer questions, using several sources and asking new related questions to learn more.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use technology, including the Internet, to produce and publish writing and present the relationships between information and ideas clearly and efficiently.
description_plain: Your child uses technology like the internet to write and share their ideas clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: With some guidance and support from peers and adults, develop and strengthen writing as needed by planning, revising, editing, rewriting, or trying a new approach, focusing on how well purpose and audience have been addressed.
description_plain: With some help, your child improves their writing by planning, revising, editing, or trying new ways to better reach their readers.

subject: Language Arts
description: Produce clear and coherent writing in which the development, organization, and style are appropriate to task, purpose, and audience.
description_plain: Your child writes clearly and in an organized way that fits the task, purpose, and audience.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide a concluding statement or section that follows from and supports the information or explanation presented.
description_plain: Your child writes a conclusion that supports the information or explanation they shared.

subject: Language Arts
description: Establish and maintain a formal style and objective tone.
description_plain: Your child writes in a formal and objective way.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use precise language and domain-specific vocabulary to inform about or explain the topic.
description_plain: Your child uses clear and specific words related to the topic to explain or inform.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use appropriate and varied transitions to create cohesion and clarify the relationships among ideas and concepts.
description_plain: Your child uses different transition words to connect ideas and make their writing easy to follow.

subject: Language Arts
description: Develop the topic with relevant, well-chosen facts, definitions, concrete details, quotations, or other information and examples.
description_plain: Your child adds facts, definitions, details, and examples to explain the topic well.

subject: Language Arts
description: Introduce a topic clearly, previewing what is to follow; organize ideas, concepts, and information into broader categories as appropriate to achieving purpose; include formatting (e.g., headings), graphics (e.g., charts, tables), and multimedia when useful to aiding comprehension.
description_plain: Your child introduces a topic clearly, organizes ideas into groups, and uses headings, charts, or pictures to help readers understand.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write informative/explanatory texts, including the narration of historical events, scientific procedures/experiments, or technical processes.
description_plain: Your child writes informative texts that explain events, scientific steps, or technical processes.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide a concluding statement or section that follows from or supports the argument presented.
description_plain: Your child writes a conclusion that supports the argument they made.

subject: Language Arts
description: Establish and maintain a formal style.
description_plain: Your child writes in a formal style.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use words, phrases, and clauses to create cohesion and clarify the relationships among claim(s), counterclaims, reasons, and evidence.
description_plain: Your child uses words and phrases to connect claims, counterclaims, reasons, and evidence clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Support claim(s) with logical reasoning and relevant, accurate data and evidence that demonstrate an understanding of the topic or text, using credible sources.
description_plain: Your child supports their claims with clear reasons and accurate facts from trustworthy sources.

subject: Language Arts
description: Introduce claim(s) about a topic or issue, acknowledge and distinguish the claim(s) from alternate or opposing claims, and organize the reasons and evidence logically.
description_plain: Your child introduces their claim about a topic, recognizes other views, and organizes reasons and evidence logically.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write arguments focused on discipline-specific content.
description_plain: Your child writes arguments focused on specific subjects.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write routinely over extended time frames (time for research, reflection, and revision) and shorter time frames (a single sitting or a day or two) for a range of discipline-specific tasks, purposes, and audiences.
description_plain: Your child writes regularly over short and long periods for different subjects, purposes, and audiences, including time for research and revising.

subject: Language Arts
description: Apply grade 7 Reading standards to literary nonfiction (e.g. "Trace and evaluate the argument and specific claims in a text, assessing whether the reasoning is sound and the evidence is relevant and sufficient to support the claims").
description_plain: Your child uses reading skills to follow and evaluate arguments and claims in nonfiction texts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Apply grade 7 Reading standards to literature (e.g., "Compare and contrast a fictional portrayal of a time, place, or character and a historical account of the same period as a means of understanding how authors of fiction use or alter history").
description_plain: Your child compares fictional stories to real historical accounts to understand how authors change history in their writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Draw evidence from literary or informational texts to support analysis, reflection, and research.
description_plain: Your child uses evidence from stories or informational texts to support their thinking, reflection, and research.

subject: Language Arts
description: Gather relevant information from multiple print and digital sources, using search terms effectively; assess the credibility and accuracy of each source; and quote or paraphrase the data and conclusions of others while avoiding plagiarism and following a standard format for citation.
description_plain: Your child finds information from different sources, checks if it’s trustworthy, and uses it correctly without copying, following citation rules.

subject: Language Arts
description: Conduct short research projects to answer a question, drawing on several sources and generating additional related, focused questions for further research and investigation.
description_plain: Your child does short research projects using several sources and asks new questions to learn more.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use technology, including the Internet, to produce and publish writing and link to and cite sources as well as to interact and collaborate with others, including linking to and citing sources.
description_plain: Your child uses technology like the Internet to write, publish, and share work, including citing sources and working with others.

subject: Language Arts
description: With some guidance and support from peers and adults, develop and strengthen writing as needed by planning, revising, editing, rewriting, or trying a new approach, focusing on how well purpose and audience have been addressed.
description_plain: With some help, your child improves their writing by planning, revising, editing, or trying new ways to make sure it fits the purpose and audience.

subject: Language Arts
description: Produce clear and coherent writing in which the development, organization, and style are appropriate to task, purpose, and audience.
description_plain: Your child can write clearly and organize their ideas so the writing fits the purpose and audience.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide a conclusion that follows from and reflects on the narrated experiences or events.
description_plain: Your child can write a conclusion that reflects on the experiences or events they described.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use precise words and phrases, relevant descriptive details, and sensory language to capture the action and convey experiences and events.
description_plain: Your child uses exact words and detailed descriptions to show actions and share experiences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use a variety of transition words, phrases, and clauses to convey sequence and signal shifts from one time frame or setting to another.
description_plain: Your child uses different transition words to show the order of events and changes in time or place.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use narrative techniques, such as dialogue, pacing, and description, to develop experiences, events, and/or characters.
description_plain: Your child uses dialogue, pacing, and descriptions to develop events and characters in their stories.

subject: Language Arts
description: Engage and orient the reader by establishing a context and point of view and introducing a narrator and/or characters; organize an event sequence that unfolds naturally and logically.
description_plain: Your child starts stories by setting the scene and introducing characters, then organizes events in a clear order.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write narratives to develop real or imagined experiences or events using effective technique, relevant descriptive details, and well-structured event sequences.
description_plain: Your child writes stories about real or imagined events using good details and clear event order.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide a concluding statement or section that follows from and supports the information or explanation presented
description_plain: Your child writes a conclusion that supports the information or explanation they gave.

subject: Language Arts
description: Establish and maintain a formal style.
description_plain: Your child keeps a formal tone when writing informational texts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use precise language and domain-specific vocabulary to inform about or explain the topic.
description_plain: Your child uses exact words and subject-specific vocabulary to explain a topic clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use appropriate transitions to create cohesion and clarify the relationships among ideas and concepts.
description_plain: Your child uses transition words to connect ideas and make their writing easy to follow.

subject: Language Arts
description: Develop the topic with relevant facts, definitions, concrete details, quotations, or other information and examples.
description_plain: Your child adds facts, definitions, details, and examples to explain their topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Introduce a topic clearly, previewing what is to follow; organize ideas, concepts, and information, using strategies such as definition, classification, comparison/contrast, and cause/effect; include formatting (e.g., headings), graphics (e.g., charts, tables), and multimedia when useful to aiding comprehension.
description_plain: Your child introduces a topic clearly and organizes information using methods like comparison or cause and effect, including helpful headings or visuals.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write informative/explanatory texts to examine a topic and convey ideas, concepts, and information through the selection, organization, and analysis of relevant content
description_plain: Your child writes informative texts that explain a topic by choosing and organizing important facts and ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide a concluding statement or section that follows from and supports the argument presented.
description_plain: Your child writes a conclusion that supports the argument they made.

subject: Language Arts
description: Establish and maintain a formal style.
description_plain: Your child keeps a formal style when writing arguments.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use words, phrases, and clauses to create cohesion and clarify the relationships among claim(s), reasons, and evidence.
description_plain: Your child uses words and phrases to connect their reasons and evidence clearly in their argument.

subject: Language Arts
description: Support claim(s) with logical reasoning and relevant evidence, using accurate, credible sources and demonstrating an understanding of the topic or text.
description_plain: Your child supports their claims with clear reasons and evidence from trustworthy sources.

subject: Language Arts
description: Introduce claim(s), acknowledge alternate or opposing claims, and organize the reasons and evidence logically.
description_plain: Your child introduces their claim, mentions opposing views, and organizes reasons and evidence logically.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write arguments to support claims with clear reasons and relevant evidence.
description_plain: Your child writes arguments that support their claims with clear reasons and evidence.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write routinely over extended time frames (time for research, reflection, and revision) and shorter time frames (a single sitting or a day or two) for a range of tasks, purposes, and audiences.
description_plain: Your child can write regularly over short and long periods for different tasks and audiences, including time to research and revise.

subject: Language Arts
description: Draw evidence from literary or informational texts to support analysis, reflection, and research.
description_plain: Your child will find and use evidence from stories or information texts to support their thinking and research.

subject: Language Arts
description: Gather relevant information from multiple print and digital sources, assess the credibility and accuracy of each source, and integrate the information while avoiding plagiarism.
description_plain: Your child can gather information from books and online sources, check if the information is trustworthy, and use it without copying.

subject: Language Arts
description: Conduct short as well as more sustained research projects based on focused questions, demonstrating understanding of the subject under investigation.
description_plain: Your child will do short and longer research projects by asking questions and showing they understand the topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use technology, including the Internet, to produce and publish writing and to interact and collaborate with others.
description_plain: Your child can use technology like the Internet to write, publish, and work with others.

subject: Language Arts
description: Develop and strengthen writing as needed by planning, revising, editing, rewriting, or trying a new approach.
description_plain: Your child will improve their writing by planning, revising, editing, or trying new ways to express ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Produce clear and coherent writing in which the development, organization, and style are appropriate to task, purpose, and audience.
description_plain: Your child can write clearly and organized for the purpose and audience they are writing for.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write narratives to develop real or imagined experiences or events using effective technique, well-chosen details, and well-structured event sequences.
description_plain: Your child will write stories about real or imagined events using good details and clear order.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write informative/explanatory texts to examine and convey complex ideas and information clearly and accurately through the effective selection, organization, and analysis of content.
description_plain: Your child can write informative texts that explain complex ideas clearly by choosing and organizing information well.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write arguments to support claims in an analysis of substantive topics or texts, using valid reasoning and relevant and sufficient evidence.
description_plain: Your child will write arguments that support their claims with good reasons and enough evidence.

subject: Language Arts
description: By the end of grade 8, read and comprehend science/technical texts in the grades 6—8 text complexity band independently and proficiently.
description_plain: Your child can read and understand science and technical texts for grades 6-8 on their own.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast the information gained from experiments, simulations, video, or multimedia sources with that gained from reading a text on the same topic.
description_plain: Your child compares information from experiments, videos, or simulations with what they read about the same topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Distinguish among facts, reasoned judgment based on research findings, and speculation in a text.
description_plain: Your child can tell the difference between facts, opinions based on research, and guesses in a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Integrate quantitative or technical information expressed in words in a text with a version of that information expressed visually (e.g., in a flowchart, diagram, model, graph, or table).
description_plain: Your child combines information written in words with pictures like charts or graphs to understand it better.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze the author's purpose in providing an explanation, describing a procedure, or discussing an experiment in a text.
description_plain: Your child figures out why the author explains something, describes a process, or talks about an experiment.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze the structure an author uses to organize a text, including how the major sections contribute to the whole and to an understanding of the topic.
description_plain: Your child looks at how the author organizes the text and how each part helps explain the topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the meaning of symbols, key terms, and other domain-specific words and phrases as they are used in a specific scientific or technical context relevant to grades 6—8 texts and topics.
description_plain: Your child understands the meaning of special words and symbols used in science or technical texts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Follow precisely a multistep procedure when carrying out experiments, taking measurements, or performing technical tasks.
description_plain: Your child follows detailed steps carefully when doing experiments or technical tasks.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the central ideas or conclusions of a text; provide an accurate summary of the text distinct from prior knowledge or opinions.
description_plain: Your child finds the main ideas or conclusions in a text and summarizes them clearly without adding personal opinions.

subject: Language Arts
description: Cite specific textual evidence to support analysis of science and technical texts.
description_plain: Your child uses specific examples from the text to support their understanding of science and technical topics.

subject: Language Arts
description: By the end of grade 8, read and comprehend history/social studies texts in the grades 6—8 text complexity band independently and proficiently.
description_plain: Your child can read and understand history and social studies texts for grades 6-8 on their own.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze the relationship between a primary and secondary source on the same topic.
description_plain: Your child compares how a primary source and a secondary source present information about the same topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Distinguish among fact, opinion, and reasoned judgment in a text.
description_plain: Your child can tell the difference between facts, opinions, and thoughtful judgments in a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Integrate visual information (e.g., in charts, graphs, photographs, videos, or maps) with other information in print and digital texts.
description_plain: Your child uses pictures, charts, or maps along with written information to understand a topic better.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify aspects of a text that reveal an author's point of view or purpose (e.g., loaded language, inclusion or avoidance of particular facts).
description_plain: Your child notices parts of a text that show the author's opinion or purpose, like strong words or missing facts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe how a text presents information (e.g., sequentially, comparatively, causally).
description_plain: Your child explains how a text organizes information, like in order, by comparison, or by cause and effect.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the meaning of words and phrases as they are used in a text, including vocabulary specific to domains related to history/social studies.
description_plain: Your child understands the meaning of words and phrases used in history and social studies texts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify key steps in a text's description of a process related to history/social studies (e.g., how a bill becomes law, how interest rates are raised or lowered).
description_plain: Your child identifies important steps in a process described in history or social studies, like how a law is made.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the central ideas or information of a primary or secondary source; provide an accurate summary of the source distinct from prior knowledge or opinions.
description_plain: Your child finds the main ideas in a primary or secondary source and summarizes them clearly without personal opinions.

subject: Language Arts
description: Cite specific textual evidence to support analysis of primary and secondary sources.
description_plain: Your child uses specific examples from history or social studies texts to support their understanding.

subject: Language Arts
description: By the end of the year, read and comprehend literary nonfiction in the grades 6–8 text complexity band proficiently, with scaffolding as needed at the high end of the range.
description_plain: Your child can read and understand nonfiction texts for grades 6–8, with help if needed for harder parts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how two or more authors writing about the same topic shape their presentations of key information by emphasizing different evidence or advancing different interpretations of facts.
description_plain: Your child compares how different authors present the same topic by focusing on different facts or ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Trace and evaluate the argument and specific claims in a text, assessing whether the reasoning is sound and the evidence is relevant and sufficient to support the claims.
description_plain: Your child follows and evaluates an author's argument to see if the reasons and evidence make sense.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast a text to an audio, video, or multimedia version of the text, analyzing each medium's portrayal of the subject (e.g., how the delivery of a speech affects the impact of the words).
description_plain: Your child compares a text with its audio, video, or multimedia version to see how each shows the subject differently.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine an author's point of view or purpose in a text and analyze how the author distinguishes his or her position from that of others.
description_plain: Your child figures out the author's point of view and how they show it is different from others.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze the structure an author uses to organize a text, including how the major sections contribute to the whole and to the development of the ideas.
description_plain: Your child looks at how an author organizes a text and how each part helps explain the ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the meaning of words and phrases as they are used in a text, including figurative, connotative, and technical meanings; analyze the impact of a specific word choice on meaning and tone.
description_plain: Your child finds the meaning of words in a text, including special or figurative meanings, and sees how word choice affects tone.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze the interactions between individuals, events, and ideas in a text (e.g., how ideas influence individuals or events, or how individuals influence ideas or events).
description_plain: Your child analyzes how people, events, and ideas in a text influence each other.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine two or more central ideas in a text and analyze their development over the course of the text; provide an objective summary of the text.
description_plain: Your child finds two or more main ideas in a text, explains how they develop, and summarizes the text fairly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Cite several pieces of textual evidence to support analysis of what the text says explicitly as well as inferences drawn from the text.
description_plain: Your child uses several pieces of evidence from the text to explain what it says and what can be inferred.

subject: Language Arts
description: By the end of the year, read and comprehend literary nonfiction in the grades 6–8 text complexity band proficiently, with scaffolding as needed at the high end of the range.
description_plain: Your child can read and understand literary nonfiction for grades 6–8, with help if needed for harder parts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast a fictional portrayal of a time, place, or character and a historical account of the same period as a means of understanding how authors of fiction use or alter history.
description_plain: Your child compares a fictional story about a time or place with a real historical account to see how fiction changes history.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast a written story, drama, or poem to its audio, filmed, staged, or multimedia version, analyzing the effects of techniques unique to each medium (e.g., lighting, sound, color, or camera focus and angles in a film).
description_plain: Your child compares a written story, play, or poem to its audio or video version to understand how each uses different techniques.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how an author develops and contrasts the points of view of different characters or narrators in a text.
description_plain: Your child analyzes how an author shows different characters' or narrators' points of view.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how a drama's or poem's form or structure (e.g., soliloquy, sonnet) contributes to its meaning.
description_plain: Your child looks at how the form or structure of a play or poem helps explain its meaning.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the meaning of words and phrases as they are used in a text, including figurative and connotative meanings; analyze the impact of rhymes and other repetitions of sounds (e.g., alliteration) on a specific verse or stanza of a poem or section of a story or drama.
description_plain: Your child finds the meaning of words and phrases in a text, including figurative language, and notices how sounds like rhymes affect a poem or story.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how particular elements of a story or drama interact (e.g., how setting shapes the characters or plot).
description_plain: Your child analyzes how parts of a story or play, like setting and characters, work together.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine a theme or central idea of a text and analyze its development over the course of the text; provide an objective summary of the text.
description_plain: Your child finds the theme or main idea of a text, explains how it develops, and summarizes the text fairly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Cite several pieces of textual evidence to support analysis of what the text says explicitly as well as inferences drawn from the text.
description_plain: Your child uses several pieces of evidence from the text to explain what it says and what can be inferred.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read and comprehend complex literary and informational texts independently and proficiently.
description_plain: Your child can read and understand difficult stories and information on their own.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how two or more texts address similar themes or topics in order to build knowledge or to compare the approaches the authors take.
description_plain: Your child can compare how two or more texts talk about the same ideas or topics and explain the different ways the authors write about them.

subject: Language Arts
description: Delineate and evaluate the argument and specific claims in a text, including the validity of the reasoning as well as the relevance and sufficiency of the evidence.
description_plain: Your child can find and think about the main points and reasons in a text and decide if the evidence is strong and relevant.

subject: Language Arts
description: Integrate and evaluate content presented in diverse media and formats, including visually and quantitatively, as well as in words.
description_plain: Your child can understand and judge information presented in different ways, like pictures, numbers, and words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Assess how point of view or purpose shapes the content and style of a text.
description_plain: Your child can explain how the author's point of view or purpose affects what is written and how it is written.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze the structure of texts, including how specific sentences, paragraphs, and larger portions of the text (e.g., a section, chapter, scene, or stanza) relate to each other and the whole.
description_plain: Your child can look at how sentences and paragraphs fit together in a text and explain how they help the whole story or article.

subject: Language Arts
description: Interpret words and phrases as they are used in a text, including determining technical, connotative, and figurative meanings, and analyze how specific word choices shape meaning or tone.
description_plain: Your child can figure out the meaning of words and phrases in a text, including special or figurative meanings, and explain how word choices affect the feeling or meaning.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how and why individuals, events, and ideas develop and interact over the course of a text.
description_plain: Your child can explain how people, events, and ideas change and connect throughout a story or text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine central ideas or themes of a text and analyze their development; summarize the key supporting details and ideas.
description_plain: Your child can find the main ideas or themes in a text, explain how they develop, and summarize the important details.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read closely to determine what the text says explicitly and to make logical inferences from it; cite specific textual evidence when writing or speaking to support conclusions drawn from the text.
description_plain: Your child can read carefully to understand what a text says and make logical guesses, using examples from the text to support their ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Acquire and use accurately grade-appropriate general academic and domain-specific words and phrases, including those that signal precise actions, emotions, or states of being (e.g., quizzed, whined, stammered) and that are basic to a particular topic (e.g., wildlife, conservation, and endangered when discussing animal preservation).
description_plain: Your child can learn and use new words that are important for school subjects and that show clear actions or feelings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of words by relating them to their opposites (antonyms) and to words with similar but not identical meanings (synonyms).
description_plain: Your child can understand words by knowing their opposites and words that mean almost the same but not exactly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recognize and explain the meaning of common idioms, adages, and proverbs.
description_plain: Your child can recognize and explain common sayings and proverbs.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain the meaning of simple similes and metaphors (e.g., as pretty as a picture) in context.
description_plain: Your child can explain simple comparisons like similes and metaphors in stories or texts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of figurative language, word relationships, and nuances in word meanings.
description_plain: Your child understands figurative language, how words relate to each other, and small differences in word meanings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Consult reference materials (e.g., dictionaries, glossaries, thesauruses), both print and digital, to find the pronunciation and determine or clarify the precise meaning of key words and phrases.
description_plain: Your child can use dictionaries and other resources to find how to say words and what they mean.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use common, grade-appropriate Greek and Latin affixes and roots as clues to the meaning of a word (e.g., telegraph, photograph, autograph).
description_plain: Your child can use parts of words from Greek and Latin to help figure out what new words mean.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use context (e.g., definitions, examples, or restatements in text) as a clue to the meaning of a word or phrase.
description_plain: Your child can use clues from the text around a word to understand what it means.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine or clarify the meaning of unknown and multiple-meaning words and phrases based on grade 4 reading and content, choosing flexibly from a range of strategies.
description_plain: Your child can figure out the meaning of new or tricky words by using different strategies.

subject: Language Arts
description: Differentiate between contexts that call for formal English (e.g., presenting ideas) and situations where informal discourse is appropriate (e.g., small-group discussion).
description_plain: Your child knows when to use formal English, like in presentations, and when informal talk is okay, like with friends.

subject: Language Arts
description: Choose punctuation for effect.
description_plain: Your child can choose punctuation marks to make writing clearer or more interesting.

subject: Language Arts
description: Choose words and phrases to convey ideas precisely.
description_plain: Your child can pick words and phrases that clearly express their ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use knowledge of language and its conventions when writing, speaking, reading, or listening.
description_plain: Your child uses what they know about language rules when writing, speaking, reading, or listening.

subject: Language Arts
description: Spell grade-appropriate words correctly, consulting references as needed.
description_plain: Your child can spell grade-level words correctly and use references when needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use a comma before a coordinating conjunction in a compound sentence.
description_plain: Your child can use a comma before words like 'and' or 'but' in sentences with two parts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use commas and quotation marks to mark direct speech and quotations from a text.
description_plain: Your child can use commas and quotation marks correctly when writing speech or quotes.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.
description_plain: Your child can use correct capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Correctly use frequently confused words (e.g., to, too, two; there, their).
description_plain: Your child can correctly use words that sound alike but have different meanings, like 'to,' 'too,' and 'two.'

subject: Language Arts
description: Produce complete sentences, recognizing and correcting inappropriate fragments and run-ons.
description_plain: Your child can write complete sentences and fix sentence fragments or run-ons.

subject: Language Arts
description: Form and use prepositional phrases.
description_plain: Your child can make and use prepositional phrases in sentences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Order adjectives within sentences according to conventional patterns (e.g., a small red bag rather than a red small bag).
description_plain: Your child can put adjectives in the right order in sentences, like 'a small red bag' instead of 'a red small bag.'

subject: Language Arts
description: Use modal auxiliaries (e.g., can, may, must) to convey various conditions.
description_plain: Your child can use helping verbs like 'can,' 'may,' and 'must' to show different meanings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Form and use the progressive (e.g., I was walking; I am walking; I will be walking) verb tenses.
description_plain: Your child can use verb forms that show actions happening now, in the past, or in the future.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use relative pronouns (who, whose, whom, which, that) and relative adverbs (where, when, why).
description_plain: Your child can use words like who, whose, whom, which, that, where, when, and why to connect ideas in sentences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English grammar and usage when writing or speaking.
description_plain: Your child understands and uses correct grammar and word usage when writing or speaking.

subject: Language Arts
description: Acquire and use accurately a range of general academic and domain-specific words and phrases sufficient for reading, writing, speaking, and listening at the college and career readiness level; demonstrate independence in gathering vocabulary knowledge when encountering an unknown term important to comprehension or expression.
description_plain: Your child learns and uses a wide range of academic and subject-specific words for reading, writing, speaking, and listening, and can find out new word meanings on their own.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of figurative language, word relationships, and nuances in word meanings.
description_plain: Your child understands figurative language, how words relate to each other, and subtle differences in word meanings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine or clarify the meaning of unknown and multiple-meaning words and phrases by using context clues, analyzing meaningful word parts, and consulting general and specialized reference materials, as appropriate.
description_plain: Your child figures out the meaning of unknown or tricky words by using clues from the sentence, breaking the word into parts, or looking them up in dictionaries or other resources.

subject: Language Arts
description: Apply knowledge of language to understand how language functions in different contexts, to make effective choices for meaning or style, and to comprehend more fully when reading or listening.
description_plain: Your child uses what they know about language to understand how it works in different situations and to choose words and styles that fit what they want to say or understand better when reading or listening.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.
description_plain: Your child uses correct capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English grammar and usage when writing or speaking.
description_plain: Your child uses correct grammar and word usage when writing or speaking.

subject: Language Arts
description: Differentiate between contexts that call for formal English (e.g., presenting ideas) and situations where informal discourse is appropriate (e.g., small-group discussion); use formal English when appropriate to task and situation.
description_plain: Your child knows when to use formal English, like during presentations, and when informal talk is okay, like in small group chats.

subject: Language Arts
description: Add audio recordings and visual displays to presentations when appropriate to enhance the development of main ideas or themes.
description_plain: Your child adds audio or pictures to presentations to help explain main ideas better.

subject: Language Arts
description: Report on a topic or text, tell a story, or recount an experience in an organized manner, using appropriate facts and relevant, descriptive details to support main ideas or themes; speak clearly at an understandable pace.
description_plain: Your child can tell about a topic, story, or experience in an organized way using facts and details, speaking clearly and at a good speed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify the reasons and evidence a speaker provides to support particular points.
description_plain: Your child can find the reasons and evidence a speaker uses to support their points.

subject: Language Arts
description: Paraphrase portions of a text read aloud or information presented in diverse media and formats, including visually, quantitatively, and orally.
description_plain: Your child can restate parts of a story or information they hear or see in their own words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Review the key ideas expressed and explain their own ideas and understanding in light of the discussion.
description_plain: Your child can explain the main ideas from a discussion and share their own thoughts about it.

subject: Language Arts
description: Pose and respond to specific questions to clarify or follow up on information, and make comments that contribute to the discussion and link to the remarks of others.
description_plain: Your child asks and answers questions to understand better and adds comments that connect to what others say during discussions.

subject: Language Arts
description: Follow agreed-upon rules for discussions and carry out assigned roles.
description_plain: Your child follows the rules for group talks and does their assigned part.

subject: Language Arts
description: Come to discussions prepared, having read or studied required material; explicitly draw on that preparation and other information known about the topic to explore ideas under discussion.
description_plain: Your child comes to discussions prepared by reading or studying and uses what they learned to share ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Engage effectively in a range of collaborative discussions (one-on-one, in groups, and teacher-led) with diverse partners on grade 4 topics and texts, building on others' ideas and expressing their own clearly.
description_plain: Your child takes part in different kinds of group talks, listens to others, and shares their own ideas clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Adapt speech to a variety of contexts and communicative tasks, demonstrating command of formal English when indicated or appropriate.
description_plain: Your child changes how they speak depending on the situation and uses formal English when needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Make strategic use of digital media and visual displays of data to express information and enhance understanding of presentations.
description_plain: Your child uses digital tools and visuals to share information and make presentations easier to understand.

subject: Language Arts
description: Present information, findings, and supporting evidence such that listeners can follow the line of reasoning and the organization, development, and style are appropriate to task, purpose, and audience.
description_plain: Your child shares information and evidence clearly so listeners can follow their ideas and the way they organize their talk fits the purpose and audience.

subject: Language Arts
description: Evaluate a speaker's point of view, reasoning, and use of evidence and rhetoric.
description_plain: Your child thinks about a speaker’s opinion, reasons, and evidence to decide how strong their argument is.

subject: Language Arts
description: Integrate and evaluate information presented in diverse media and formats, including visually, quantitatively, and orally.
description_plain: Your child combines and evaluates information from different sources like pictures, numbers, and spoken words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Prepare for and participate effectively in a range of conversations and collaborations with diverse partners, building on others' ideas and expressing their own clearly and persuasively.
description_plain: Your child gets ready for and takes part in conversations with different people, building on others’ ideas and sharing their own clearly and confidently.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write routinely over extended time frames (time for research, reflection, and revision) and shorter time frames (a single sitting or a day or two) for a range of discipline-specific tasks, purposes, and audiences.
description_plain: Your child writes regularly over short and long periods for different subjects, reasons, and audiences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Apply grade 4 Reading standards to informational texts (e.g., "Explain how an author uses reasons and evidence to support particular points in a text").
description_plain: Your child uses reading skills to explain how authors support points with reasons and evidence in informational texts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Apply grade 4 Reading standards to literature (e.g., "Describe in depth a character, setting, or event in a story or drama, drawing on specific details in the text [e.g., a character's thoughts, words, or actions].").
description_plain: Your child uses reading skills to describe characters, settings, or events in stories by using details from the text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Draw evidence from literary or informational texts to support analysis, reflection, and research.
description_plain: Your child finds evidence from stories or informational texts to support their thinking, reflection, and research.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recall relevant information from experiences or gather relevant information from print and digital sources; take notes and categorize information, and provide a list of sources.
description_plain: Your child remembers or finds information from books or online, takes notes, organizes it, and lists where they got it.

subject: Language Arts
description: Conduct short research projects that build knowledge through investigation of different aspects of a topic.
description_plain: Your child does short research projects to learn about different parts of a topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: With some guidance and support from adults, use technology, including the Internet, to produce and publish writing as well as to interact and collaborate with others; demonstrate sufficient command of keyboarding skills to type a minimum of one page in a single sitting.
description_plain: With some help, your child uses technology like the Internet to write, publish, and work with others, and can type at least one page in one sitting.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support from peers and adults, develop and strengthen writing as needed by planning, revising, and editing.
description_plain: With help from others, your child plans, revises, and edits their writing to make it better.

subject: Language Arts
description: Produce clear and coherent writing in which the development and organization are appropriate to task, purpose, and audience.
description_plain: Your child writes clearly and organizes their ideas to fit the task, purpose, and audience.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide a conclusion that follows from the narrated experiences or events.
description_plain: Your child writes a conclusion that follows from the experiences or events they described.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use concrete words and phrases and sensory details to convey experiences and events precisely.
description_plain: Your child uses clear words and sensory details to describe experiences and events.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use a variety of transitional words and phrases to manage the sequence of events.
description_plain: Your child uses different transition words to show the order of events.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use dialogue and description to develop experiences and events or show the responses of characters to situations.
description_plain: Your child uses dialogue and descriptions to show experiences and how characters react.

subject: Language Arts
description: Orient the reader by establishing a situationand introducing a narrator and/or characters; organize an event sequence that unfolds naturally.
description_plain: Your child starts stories by setting the scene and introducing characters, then tells events in a natural order.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write narratives to develop real or imagined experiences or events using effective technique, descriptive details, and clear event sequences.
description_plain: Your child writes stories about real or imagined events using good techniques, details, and clear order.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide a concluding statement or section related to the information or explanation presented
description_plain: Your child writes a closing statement or section that relates to the information or explanation they gave.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use precise language and domain-specific vocabulary to inform about or explain the topic.
description_plain: Your child can use clear and specific words related to the topic to explain or share information.

subject: Language Arts
description: Link ideas within categories of information using words and phrases (e.g., another, for example, also, because).
description_plain: Your child will connect ideas using words like 'another,' 'for example,' and 'because' to make writing flow.

subject: Language Arts
description: Develop the topic with facts, definitions, concrete details, quotations, or other information and examples related to the topic.
description_plain: Your child can add facts, definitions, details, or examples to explain a topic clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Introduce a topic clearly and group related information in paragraphs and sections; include formatting (e.g., headings), illustrations, and multimedia when useful to aiding comprehension.
description_plain: Your child will start writing by clearly introducing the topic and organizing information into paragraphs, using headings or pictures when helpful.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write informative/explanatory texts to examine a topic and convey ideas and information clearly.
description_plain: Your child can write informative texts that explain a topic and share ideas clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide a concluding statement or section related to the opinion presented.
description_plain: Your child will write a closing sentence or section that relates to their opinion.

subject: Language Arts
description: Link opinion and reasons using words and phrases (e.g., for instance, in order to, in addition).
description_plain: Your child can connect their opinion and reasons using words like 'for instance' or 'in addition.'

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide reasons that are supported by facts and details.
description_plain: Your child will give reasons for their opinion that are supported by facts and details.

subject: Language Arts
description: Introduce a topic or text clearly, state an opinion, and create an organizational structure in which related ideas are grouped to support the writer's purpose.
description_plain: Your child can clearly introduce a topic, state their opinion, and organize ideas to support their point.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write opinion pieces on topics or texts, supporting a point of view with reasons and information
description_plain: Your child will write opinion pieces that share their point of view with reasons and information.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write routinely over extended time frames (time for research, reflection, and revision) and shorter time frames (a single sitting or a day or two) for a range of tasks, purposes, and audiences.
description_plain: Your child can write regularly over short and long periods for different tasks and audiences, including time to research and revise.

subject: Language Arts
description: Draw evidence from literary or informational texts to support analysis, reflection, and research.
description_plain: Your child will find and use evidence from stories or information texts to support their thinking and research.

subject: Language Arts
description: Gather relevant information from multiple print and digital sources, assess the credibility and accuracy of each source, and integrate the information while avoiding plagiarism.
description_plain: Your child can gather information from books and online sources, check if the information is trustworthy, and use it without copying.

subject: Language Arts
description: Conduct short as well as more sustained research projects based on focused questions, demonstrating understanding of the subject under investigation.
description_plain: Your child will do short and longer research projects by asking questions and showing they understand the topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use technology, including the Internet, to produce and publish writing and to interact and collaborate with others.
description_plain: Your child can use technology like the Internet to write, publish, and work with others.

subject: Language Arts
description: Develop and strengthen writing as needed by planning, revising, editing, rewriting, or trying a new approach.
description_plain: Your child will improve their writing by planning, revising, editing, or trying new ways to express ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Produce clear and coherent writing in which the development, organization, and style are appropriate to task, purpose, and audience.
description_plain: Your child can write clearly and organized for the purpose and audience they are writing for.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write narratives to develop real or imagined experiences or events using effective technique, well-chosen details, and well-structured event sequences.
description_plain: Your child will write stories about real or imagined events using good details and clear order.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write informative/explanatory texts to examine and convey complex ideas and information clearly and accurately through the effective selection, organization, and analysis of content.
description_plain: Your child can write informative texts that explain complex ideas clearly by choosing and organizing information well.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write arguments to support claims in an analysis of substantive topics or texts, using valid reasoning and relevant and sufficient evidence.
description_plain: Your child will write arguments that support their claims with good reasons and enough evidence.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use context to confirm or self-correct word recognition and understanding, rereading as necessary.
description_plain: Your child can use clues from the sentence to figure out or fix words they don’t know, rereading if needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read on-level prose and poetry orally with accuracy, appropriate rate, and expression on successive readings
description_plain: Your child will read stories and poems out loud with accuracy, good speed, and expression.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read on-level text with purpose and understanding.
description_plain: Your child can read grade-level text with a clear purpose and understanding.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read with sufficient accuracy and fluency to support comprehension.
description_plain: Your child will read accurately and smoothly enough to understand what they read.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use combined knowledge of all letter-sound correspondences, syllabication patterns, and morphology (e.g., roots and affixes) to read accurately unfamiliar multisyllabic words in context and out of context.
description_plain: Your child can use knowledge of letter sounds, syllables, and word parts to read new longer words correctly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Know and apply grade-level phonics and word analysis skills in decoding words.
description_plain: Your child will use grade-level phonics and word skills to sound out and understand words.

subject: Language Arts
description: By the end of year, read and comprehend informational texts, including history/social studies, science, and technical texts, in the grades 4—5 text complexity band proficiently, with scaffolding as needed at the high end of the range.
description_plain: By the end of fourth grade, your child can read and understand informational texts about history, science, and technical subjects with some help for harder parts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Integrate information from two texts on the same topic in order to write or speak about the subject knowledgeably.
description_plain: Your child can combine information from two texts on the same topic to talk or write about it knowledgeably.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain how an author uses reasons and evidence to support particular points in a text.
description_plain: Your child will explain how an author uses reasons and evidence to support points in a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Interpret information presented visually, orally, or quantitatively (e.g., in charts, graphs, diagrams, time lines, animations, or interactive elements on Web pages) and explain how the information contributes to an understanding of the text in which it appears.
description_plain: Your child can understand and explain information shown in pictures, charts, or graphs and how it helps explain the text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast a firsthand and secondhand account of the same event or topic; describe the differences in focus and the information provided.
description_plain: Your child will compare a firsthand and secondhand account of the same event and describe how they are different.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe the overall structure (e.g., chronology, comparison, cause/effect, problem/solution) of events, ideas, concepts, or information in a text or part of a text.
description_plain: Your child can describe how a text is organized, like by time order, cause and effect, or problem and solution.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the meaning of general academic and domain-specific words or phrases in a text relevant to a grade 4 topic or subject area.
description_plain: Your child will figure out the meaning of important words related to grade 4 topics in a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain events, procedures, ideas, or concepts in a historical, scientific, or technical text, including what happened and why, based on specific information in the text.
description_plain: Your child can explain events or ideas in history, science, or technical texts, including what happened and why.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the main idea of a text and explain how it is supported by key details; summarize the text.
description_plain: Your child will find the main idea of a text, explain how details support it, and summarize the text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Refer to details and examples in a text when explaining what the text says explicitly and when drawing inferences from the text.
description_plain: Your child can use details and examples from a text to explain what it says and to make inferences.

subject: Language Arts
description: By the end of the year, read and comprehend literature, including stories, dramas, and poetry, in the grades 4—5 text complexity band proficiently, with scaffolding as needed at the high end of the range.
description_plain: By the end of fourth grade, your child can read and understand stories, plays, and poems at their grade level, with some help for harder parts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast the treatment of similar themes and topics (e.g., opposition of good and evil) and patterns of events (e.g., the quest) in stories, myths, and traditional literature from different cultures.
description_plain: Your child will compare how similar themes and story patterns appear in stories and myths from different cultures.

subject: Language Arts
description: Make connections between the text of a story or drama and a visual or oral presentation of the text, identifying where each version reflects specific descriptions and directions in the text.
description_plain: Your child can connect a story or play to its visual or oral presentation by identifying where the presentation matches the text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast the point of view from which different stories are narrated, including the difference between first- and third-person narrations.
description_plain: Your child will compare different points of view in stories, including the difference between first-person and third-person narrations.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain major differences between poems, drama, and prose, and refer to the structural elements of poems (e.g., verse, rhythm, meter) and drama (e.g., casts of characters, settings, descriptions, dialogue, stage directions) when writing or speaking about a text.
description_plain: Your child can tell the main differences between poems, plays, and stories, and talk about parts of poems like rhythm and parts of plays like characters and dialogue.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the meaning of words and phrases as they are used in a text, including those that allude to significant characters found in mythology (e.g., Herculean).
description_plain: Your child can figure out what words and phrases mean in a story, including words that refer to famous characters from myths.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe in depth a character, setting, or event in a story or drama, drawing on specific details in the text (e.g., a character's thoughts, words, or actions).
description_plain: Your child can describe a character, place, or event in a story using details like what a character thinks, says, or does.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine a theme of a story, drama, or poem from details in the text; summarize the text.
description_plain: Your child can find the main message or theme of a story, play, or poem and tell what the text is about in a short summary.

subject: Language Arts
description: Refer to details and examples in a text when explaining what the text says explicitly and when drawing inferences from the text.
description_plain: Your child can use examples from a story to explain what it says clearly and to make guesses about what it means.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read and comprehend complex literary and informational texts independently and proficiently.
description_plain: Your child can read and understand difficult stories and information on their own.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how two or more texts address similar themes or topics in order to build knowledge or to compare the approaches the authors take.
description_plain: Your child can compare how two or more texts talk about the same ideas or topics and explain the different ways the authors write about them.

subject: Language Arts
description: Delineate and evaluate the argument and specific claims in a text, including the validity of the reasoning as well as the relevance and sufficiency of the evidence.
description_plain: Your child can find and think about the main points and reasons in a text and decide if the evidence is strong and relevant.

subject: Language Arts
description: Integrate and evaluate content presented in diverse media and formats, including visually and quantitatively, as well as in words.
description_plain: Your child can understand and judge information presented in different ways, like pictures, numbers, and words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Assess how point of view or purpose shapes the content and style of a text.
description_plain: Your child can explain how the author's point of view or purpose affects what is written and how it is written.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze the structure of texts, including how specific sentences, paragraphs, and larger portions of the text (e.g., a section, chapter, scene, or stanza) relate to each other and the whole.
description_plain: Your child can look at how sentences and paragraphs fit together in a text and explain how they help the whole story or article.

subject: Language Arts
description: Interpret words and phrases as they are used in a text, including determining technical, connotative, and figurative meanings, and analyze how specific word choices shape meaning or tone.
description_plain: Your child can figure out the meaning of words and phrases in a text, including special or figurative meanings, and explain how word choices affect the feeling or meaning.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how and why individuals, events, and ideas develop and interact over the course of a text.
description_plain: Your child can explain how people, events, and ideas change and connect throughout a story or text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine central ideas or themes of a text and analyze their development; summarize the key supporting details and ideas.
description_plain: Your child can find the main ideas or themes in a text, explain how they develop, and summarize the important details.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read closely to determine what the text says explicitly and to make logical inferences from it; cite specific textual evidence when writing or speaking to support conclusions drawn from the text.
description_plain: Your child can read carefully to understand what a text says and make logical guesses, using examples from the text to support their ideas.

subject: Math
description: Solve real world problems involving division of unit fractions by non-zero whole numbers and division of whole numbers by unit fractions, e.g., by using visual fraction models and equations to represent the problem.
description_plain: Your child can solve real problems dividing unit fractions by whole numbers and whole numbers by unit fractions using pictures or equations.

subject: Math
description: Interpret division of a whole number by a unit fraction, and compute such quotients.
description_plain: Your child can understand and calculate what happens when you divide a whole number by a unit fraction.

subject: Math
description: Interpret division of a unit fraction by a non-zero whole number, and compute such quotients.
description_plain: Your child can understand and calculate what happens when you divide a unit fraction by a whole number.

subject: Math
description: Apply and extend previous understandings of division to divide unit fractions by whole numbers and whole numbers by unit fractions.
description_plain: Your child can divide unit fractions by whole numbers and whole numbers by unit fractions, building on what they already know about division.

subject: Math
description: Solve real world problems involving multiplication of fractions and mixed numbers, e.g., by using visual fraction models or equations to represent the problem.
description_plain: Your child can solve real problems multiplying fractions and mixed numbers using pictures or equations.

subject: Math
description: Explaining why multiplying a given number by a fraction greater than 1 results in a product greater than the given number (recognizing multiplication by whole numbers greater than 1 as a familiar case); explaining why multiplying a given number by a fraction less than 1 results in a product smaller than the given number; and relating the principle of fraction equivalence a/b = (n×a)/(n×b) to the effect of multiplying a/b by 1.
description_plain: Your child can explain why multiplying by a fraction bigger than 1 makes a number bigger, and multiplying by a fraction less than 1 makes it smaller, and understand multiplying fractions by 1 keeps them the same.

subject: Math
description: Comparing the size of a product to the size of one factor on the basis of the size of the other factor, without performing the indicated multiplication.
description_plain: Your child can compare the size of a multiplication result to one factor by looking at the other factor without actually multiplying.

subject: Math
description: Interpret multiplication as scaling (resizing), by:
description_plain: Your child understands multiplication as resizing or scaling.

subject: Math
description: Find the area of a rectangle with fractional side lengths by tiling it with unit squares of the appropriate unit fraction side lengths, and show that the area is the same as would be found by multiplying the side lengths. Multiply fractional side lengths to find areas of rectangles, and represent fraction products as rectangular areas.
description_plain: Your child can find the area of rectangles with fractional side lengths by counting small squares and by multiplying the side lengths.

subject: Math
description: Interpret the product (a/b) × q as a parts of a partition of q into b equal parts; equivalently, as the result of a sequence of operations a × q ÷ b.
description_plain: Your child understands multiplying a fraction by a whole number as taking parts of that whole number or by multiplying and dividing in steps.

subject: Math
description: Apply and extend previous understandings of multiplication to multiply a fraction or whole number by a fraction.
description_plain: Your child can multiply a fraction by another fraction or by a whole number.

subject: Math
description: Interpret a fraction as division of the numerator by the denominator (a/b = a ÷ b). Solve word problems involving division of whole numbers leading to answers in the form of fractions or mixed numbers, e.g., by using visual fraction models or equations to represent the problem.
description_plain: Your child understands a fraction as dividing the top number by the bottom number and can solve word problems using this idea.

subject: Math
description: Apply and extend previous understandings of multiplication and division to multiply and divide fractions.
description_plain: Your child can multiply and divide fractions and whole numbers.

subject: Math
description: Solve word problems involving addition and subtraction of fractions referring to the same whole, including cases of unlike denominators, e.g., by using visual fraction models or equations to represent the problem. Use benchmark fractions and number sense of fractions to estimate mentally and assess the reasonableness of answers.
description_plain: Your child can solve word problems adding and subtracting fractions with different denominators using pictures or equations and can estimate if answers make sense.

subject: Math
description: Add and subtract fractions with unlike denominators (including mixed numbers) by replacing given fractions with equivalent fractions in such a way as to produce an equivalent sum or difference of fractions with like denominators.
description_plain: Your child can add and subtract fractions with different denominators by changing them to have the same denominator first.

subject: Math
description: Use equivalent fractions as a strategy to add and subtract fractions.
description_plain: Your child uses equivalent fractions to help add and subtract fractions.

subject: Math
description: Classify two-dimensional figures in a hierarchy based on properties.
description_plain: Your child can sort flat shapes into groups based on their properties, like sides and angles.

subject: Math
description: Understand that attributes belonging to a category of two-dimensional figures also belong to all subcategories of that category.
description_plain: Your child understands that if a shape belongs to a big group, it also has the properties of smaller groups inside it.

subject: Math
description: Classify two-dimensional figures into categories based on their properties.
description_plain: Your child can put flat shapes into categories by looking at their features.

subject: Math
description: Represent real world and mathematical problems by graphing points in the first quadrant of the coordinate plane, and interpret coordinate values of points in the context of the situation.
description_plain: Your child can plot points on a graph in the first quadrant and explain what the points mean in real situations.

subject: Math
description: Use a pair of perpendicular number lines, called axes, to define a coordinate system, with the intersection of the lines (the origin) arranged to coincide with the 0 on each line and a given point in the plane located by using an ordered pair of numbers, called its coordinates. Understand that the first number indicates how far to travel from the origin in the direction of one axis, and the second number indicates how far to travel in the direction of the second axis, with the convention that the names of the two axes and the coordinates correspond (e.g., x-axis and x-coordinate, y-axis and y-coordinate).
description_plain: Your child understands how to use two number lines that cross at zero to find points using pairs of numbers called coordinates.

subject: Math
description: Graph points on the coordinate plane to solve real-world and mathematical problems.
description_plain: Your child can graph points on a coordinate plane to solve problems.

subject: Math
description: Recognize volume as additive. Find volumes of solid figures composed of two non-overlapping right rectangular prisms by adding the volumes of the non-overlapping parts, applying this technique to solve real world problems.
description_plain: Your child can find the volume of solid shapes made by joining two rectangular boxes by adding their volumes.

subject: Math
description: Apply the formulas V = l × w × h and V = b × h for rectangular prisms to find volumes of right rectangular prisms with whole-number edge lengths in the context of solving real world and mathematical problems.
description_plain: Your child can use formulas to find the volume of rectangular boxes with whole number sides in real problems.

subject: Math
description: Find the volume of a right rectangular prism with whole-number side lengths by packing it with unit cubes, and show that the volume is the same as would be found by multiplying the edge lengths, equivalently by multiplying the height by the area of the base. Represent threefold whole-number products as volumes, e.g., to represent the associative property of multiplication.
description_plain: Your child can find the volume of a rectangular box by filling it with unit cubes and by multiplying its length, width, and height.

subject: Math
description: Relate volume to the operations of multiplication and addition and solve real world and mathematical problems involving volume.
description_plain: Your child understands volume relates to multiplication and addition and can solve problems about volume.

subject: Math
description: Measure volumes by counting unit cubes, using cubic cm, cubic in, cubic ft, and improvised units.
description_plain: Your child can measure volume by counting unit cubes using different units like cubic centimeters or cubic inches.

subject: Math
description: A solid figure which can be packed without gaps or overlaps using n unit cubes is said to have a volume of n cubic units.
description_plain: Your child knows that a solid made by fitting together n unit cubes has a volume of n cubic units.

subject: Math
description: A cube with side length 1 unit, called a "unit cube," is said to have "one cubic unit" of volume, and can be used to measure volume.
description_plain: Your child understands a cube with sides one unit long is called a unit cube and has a volume of one cubic unit.

subject: Math
description: Recognize volume as an attribute of solid figures and understand concepts of volume measurement.
description_plain: Your child recognizes volume as a property of solid shapes and understands how to measure it.

subject: Math
description: Geometric measurement: understand concepts of volume and relate volume to multiplication and to addition.
description_plain: Your child understands volume and how it connects to multiplication and addition.

subject: Math
description: Make a line plot to display a data set of measurements in fractions of a unit (1/2, 1/4, 1/8). Use operations on fractions for this grade to solve problems involving information presented in line plots.
description_plain: Your child can make a line plot to show measurements with fractions and use fraction math to solve problems from the plot.

subject: Math
description: Convert among different-sized standard measurement units within a given measurement system (e.g., convert 5 cm to 0.05 m), and use these conversions in solving multi-step, real world problems.
description_plain: Your child can change measurements from one unit to another within the same system and use these changes to solve multi-step problems.

subject: Math
description: Convert like measurement units within a given measurement system.
description_plain: Your child can convert between different units of measurement within the same system.

subject: Math
description: Add, subtract, multiply, and divide decimals to hundredths, using concrete models or drawings and strategies based on place value, properties of operations, and/or the relationship between addition and subtraction; relate the strategy to a written method and explain the reasoning used.
description_plain: Your child can add, subtract, multiply, and divide decimals to hundredths using models and strategies, and explain how they solved the problems.

subject: Math
