description: Explain why a fraction a/b is equivalent to a fraction (n × a)/(n × b) by using visual fraction models, with attention to how the number and size of the parts differ even though the two fractions themselves are the same size. Use this principle to recognize and generate equivalent fractions.
description_plain: Your child can explain why multiplying the numerator and denominator of a fraction by the same number makes an equivalent fraction using pictures, and use this to find equivalent fractions.

subject: Math
description: Extend understanding of fraction equivalence and ordering.
description_plain: Your child understands that some fractions are equal and can put fractions in order from smallest to largest.

subject: Math
description: Recognize a line of symmetry for a two-dimensional figure as a line across the figure such that the figure can be folded along the line into matching parts. Identify line-symmetric figures and draw lines of symmetry.
description_plain: Your child can find lines of symmetry in flat shapes by folding them to see if the parts match, and can draw these lines.

subject: Math
description: Classify two-dimensional figures based on the presence or absence of parallel or perpendicular lines, or the presence or absence of angles of a specified size. Recognize right triangles as a category, and identify right triangles.
description_plain: Your child can sort flat shapes by whether they have parallel or perpendicular lines or certain angle sizes, and can recognize right triangles.

subject: Math
description: Draw points, lines, line segments, rays, angles (right, acute, obtuse), and perpendicular and parallel lines. Identify these in two-dimensional figures.
description_plain: Your child can draw points, lines, line segments, rays, different types of angles, and parallel or perpendicular lines, and find these in shapes.

subject: Math
description: Draw and identify lines and angles, and classify shapes by properties of their lines and angles.
description_plain: Your child can draw and recognize lines and angles, and group shapes based on their lines and angles.

subject: Math
description: Recognize angle measure as additive. When an angle is decomposed into non-overlapping parts, the angle measure of the whole is the sum of the angle measures of the parts. Solve addition and subtraction problems to find unknown angles on a diagram in real world and mathematical problems, e.g., by using an equation with a symbol for the unknown angle measure.
description_plain: Your child understands that the size of an angle is the sum of its parts and can solve problems to find missing angles using addition and subtraction.

subject: Math
description: Measure angles in whole-number degrees using a protractor. Sketch angles of specified measure.
description_plain: Your child can measure angles in whole degrees using a protractor and draw angles with a given size.

subject: Math
description: An angle that turns through n one-degree angles is said to have an angle measure of n degrees.
description_plain: Your child knows that an angle made by turning through n one-degree steps has a measure of n degrees.

subject: Math
description: An angle is measured with reference to a circle with its center at the common endpoint of the rays, by considering the fraction of the circular arc between the points where the two rays intersect the circle. An angle that turns through 1/360 of a circle is called a "one-degree angle," and can be used to measure angles.
description_plain: Your child understands that angles are measured by how much of a circle they cover, with one degree being 1/360 of a full circle.

subject: Math
description: Recognize angles as geometric shapes that are formed wherever two rays share a common endpoint, and understand concepts of angle measurement:
description_plain: Your child knows that angles are shapes made by two rays sharing a point and understands how to measure them.

subject: Math
description: Geometric measurement: understand concepts of angle and measure angles.
description_plain: Your child understands what angles are and how to measure them.

subject: Math
description: Make a line plot to display a data set of measurements in fractions of a unit (1/2, 1/4, 1/8). Solve problems involving addition and subtraction of fractions by using information presented in line plots.
description_plain: Your child can make a line plot to show measurements using fractions like 1/2, 1/4, or 1/8 and solve addition and subtraction problems using the plot.

subject: Math
description: Apply the area and perimeter formulas for rectangles in real world and mathematical problems.
description_plain: Your child can use formulas to find the area and perimeter of rectangles in real-life and math problems.

subject: Math
description: Use the four operations to solve word problems involving distances, intervals of time, liquid volumes, masses of objects, and money, including problems involving simple fractions or decimals, and problems that require expressing measurements given in a larger unit in terms of a smaller unit. Represent measurement quantities using diagrams such as number line diagrams that feature a measurement scale.
description_plain: Your child can solve word problems using addition, subtraction, multiplication, and division with measurements like distance, time, volume, weight, and money, including fractions and decimals, and can show measurements on number lines.

subject: Math
description: Know relative sizes of measurement units within one system of units including km, m, cm; kg, g; lb, oz.; l, ml; hr, min, sec. Within a single system of measurement, express measurements in a larger unit in terms of a smaller unit. Record measurement equivalents in a two column table.
description_plain: Your child knows how big different measurement units are within one system (like kilometers, meters, centimeters) and can change measurements from bigger units to smaller units and record these conversions in a table.

subject: Math
description: Solve problems involving measurement and conversion of measurements from a larger unit to a smaller unit.
description_plain: Your child can solve problems that involve measuring and changing measurements from bigger units to smaller units.

subject: Math
description: Find whole-number quotients and remainders with up to four-digit dividends and one-digit divisors, using strategies based on place value, the properties of operations, and/or the relationship between multiplication and division. Illustrate and explain the calculation by using equations, rectangular arrays, and/or area models.
description_plain: Your child can divide numbers up to four digits by one-digit numbers using place value and multiplication/division facts, and explain their work with equations or pictures.

subject: Math
description: Multiply a whole number of up to four digits by a one-digit whole number, and multiply two two-digit numbers, using strategies based on place value and the properties of operations. Illustrate and explain the calculation by using equations, rectangular arrays, and/or area models.
description_plain: Your child can multiply a number up to four digits by a one-digit number and multiply two two-digit numbers using place value and properties of multiplication, and explain their work with equations or pictures.

subject: Math
description: Fluently add and subtract multi-digit whole numbers using the standard algorithm.
description_plain: Your child can quickly add and subtract large whole numbers using the standard method.

subject: Math
description: Use place value understanding and properties of operations to perform multi-digit arithmetic.
description_plain: Your child uses place value and math rules to add, subtract, multiply, and divide multi-digit numbers.

subject: Math
description: Use place value understanding to round multi-digit whole numbers to any place.
description_plain: Your child can round multi-digit whole numbers to any place value.

subject: Math
description: Read and write multi-digit whole numbers using base-ten numerals, number names, and expanded form. Compare two multi-digit numbers based on meanings of the digits in each place, using >, =, and < symbols to record the results of comparisons.
description_plain: Your child can read and write large whole numbers in digits, words, and expanded form, and compare two numbers using >, =, or < based on their digits.

subject: Math
description: Recognize that in a multi-digit whole number, a digit in one place represents ten times what it represents in the place to its right.
description_plain: Your child understands that in a multi-digit number, a digit in one place is worth ten times what it is in the place to its right.

subject: Math
description: Generalize place value understanding for multi-digit whole numbers.
description_plain: Your child understands place value for large whole numbers.

subject: Math
description: Generate a number or shape pattern that follows a given rule. Identify apparent features of the pattern that were not explicit in the rule itself.
description_plain: Your child can create number or shape patterns that follow a rule and notice features in the pattern that the rule doesn't directly say.

subject: Math
description: Generate and analyze patterns.
description_plain: Your child can make and study patterns.

subject: Math
description: Find all factor pairs for a whole number in the range 1—100. Recognize that a whole number is a multiple of each of its factors. Determine whether a given whole number in the range 1—100 is a multiple of a given one-digit number. Determine whether a given whole number in the range 1—100 is prime or composite.
description_plain: Your child can find all factor pairs of numbers from 1 to 100, know that numbers are multiples of their factors, decide if a number is a multiple of a one-digit number, and tell if a number is prime or composite.

subject: Math
description: Gain familiarity with factors and multiples.
description_plain: Your child understands factors and multiples.

subject: Math
description: Solve multistep word problems posed with whole numbers and having whole-number answers using the four operations, including problems in which remainders must be interpreted. Represent these problems using equations with a letter standing for the unknown quantity. Assess the reasonableness of answers using mental computation and estimation strategies including rounding.
description_plain: Your child can solve word problems with several steps using addition, subtraction, multiplication, or division, including problems with remainders, write equations with letters for unknowns, and check if answers make sense by estimating.

subject: Math
description: Multiply or divide to solve word problems involving multiplicative comparison, e.g., by using drawings and equations with a symbol for the unknown number to represent the problem, distinguishing multiplicative comparison from additive comparison.
description_plain: Your child can solve word problems comparing amounts by multiplying or dividing, using drawings and equations with symbols for unknown numbers, and tell the difference between multiplying and adding comparisons.

subject: Math
description: Interpret a multiplication equation as a comparison, e.g., interpret 35 = 5 × 7 as a statement that 35 is 5 times as many as 7 and 7 times as many as 5. Represent verbal statements of multiplicative comparisons as multiplication equations.
description_plain: Your child understands multiplication equations as comparisons, like seeing 35 = 5 × 7 as 35 is 5 times 7, and can write multiplication equations from words about comparisons.

subject: Math
description: Use the four operations with whole numbers to solve problems.
description_plain: Your child can use addition, subtraction, multiplication, and division with whole numbers to solve problems.

subject: Math
description: Look for and express regularity in repeated reasoning.
description_plain: Your child can notice and explain patterns when solving problems repeatedly.

subject: Math
description: Look for and make use of structure.
description_plain: Your child can find and use the way things are organized or structured to help solve problems.

subject: Math
description: Use appropriate tools strategically.
description_plain: Your child can choose and use the right tools to help solve math problems.

subject: Math
description: Construct viable arguments and critique the reasoning of others.
description_plain: Your child can explain their thinking clearly and listen to others' ideas to decide if they make sense.

subject: Math
description: Reason abstractly and quantitatively.
description_plain: Your child can think about math problems both in general ideas and with real numbers.

subject: Math
description: Make sense of problems and persevere in solving them.
description_plain: Your child can understand problems and keep trying until they find a solution.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use words and phrases acquired through conversations, reading and being read to, and responding to texts, including using adjectives and adverbs to describe (e.g., When other kids are happy that makes me happy).
description_plain: Your child can use new words and phrases learned from talking, reading, and listening, including describing things with adjectives and adverbs.

subject: Language Arts
description: Distinguish shades of meaning among closely related verbs (e.g., toss, throw, hurl) and closely related adjectives (e.g., thin, slender, skinny, scrawny).
description_plain: Your child can tell the difference between similar verbs (like toss, throw, hurl) and similar adjectives (like thin, slender, skinny, scrawny).

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify real-life connections between words and their use (e.g., describe foods that are spicy or juicy).
description_plain: Your child can connect words to real-life examples, like describing foods that are spicy or juicy.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of word relationships and nuances in word meanings.
description_plain: Your child understands how words relate to each other and can notice small differences in word meanings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use glossaries and beginning dictionaries, both print and digital, to determine or clarify the meaning of words and phrases.
description_plain: Your child can use glossaries and simple dictionaries, both in books and online, to find or check the meanings of words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use knowledge of the meaning of individual words to predict the meaning of compound words (e.g., birdhouse, lighthouse, housefly; bookshelf, notebook, bookmark).
description_plain: Your child can figure out the meaning of compound words by understanding the meanings of the smaller words inside them.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use a known root word as a clue to the meaning of an unknown word with the same root (e.g., addition, additional).
description_plain: Your child can use a familiar root word to help understand the meaning of a new word that has the same root.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the meaning of the new word formed when a known prefix is added to a known word (e.g., happy/unhappy, tell/retell).
description_plain: Your child can figure out the meaning of a new word by knowing the meaning of a prefix added to a familiar word.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use sentence-level context as a clue to the meaning of a word or phrase.
description_plain: Your child can use the sentence around a word to help understand what that word or phrase means.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine or clarify the meaning of unknown and multiple-meaning words and phrases based on grade 2 reading and content, choosing flexibly from an array of strategies.
description_plain: Your child can find or understand the meaning of new or tricky words using different strategies based on what they read and learn.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare formal and informal uses of English.
description_plain: Your child can tell the difference between formal and informal ways of speaking and writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use knowledge of language and its conventions when writing, speaking, reading, or listening.
description_plain: Your child uses what they know about language rules when writing, speaking, reading, or listening.

subject: Language Arts
description: Consult reference materials, including beginning dictionaries, as needed to check and correct spellings.
description_plain: Your child can use reference books or dictionaries to check and fix spelling when needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Generalize learned spelling patterns when writing words (e.g., cage → badge; boy → boil).
description_plain: Your child can use spelling patterns they’ve learned to help spell new words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use an apostrophe to form contractions and frequently occurring possessives.
description_plain: Your child can use apostrophes correctly to make contractions and show possession.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use commas in greetings and closings of letters.
description_plain: Your child can use commas correctly in greetings and closings of letters.

subject: Language Arts
description: Capitalize holidays, product names, and geographic names.
description_plain: Your child knows to capitalize holidays, brand names, and place names.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.
description_plain: Your child can use correct capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Produce, expand, and rearrange complete simple and compound sentences (e.g., The boy watched the movie; The little boy watched the movie; The action movie was watched by the little boy).
description_plain: Your child can write and change simple and compound sentences to make their meaning clearer.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use adjectives and adverbs, and choose between them depending on what is to be modified.
description_plain: Your child can use adjectives and adverbs correctly and choose the right one to describe something.

subject: Language Arts
description: Form and use the past tense of frequently occurring irregular verbs (e.g., sat, hid, told).
description_plain: Your child can use the past tense of common irregular verbs like sat, hid, and told.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use reflexive pronouns (e.g., myself, ourselves).
description_plain: Your child can use reflexive pronouns like myself and ourselves correctly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Form and use frequently occurring irregular plural nouns (e.g., feet, children, teeth, mice, fish).
description_plain: Your child can use irregular plural nouns like feet, children, teeth, mice, and fish.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use collective nouns (e.g., group).
description_plain: Your child can use collective nouns like group.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English grammar and usage when writing or speaking.
description_plain: Your child understands and uses correct grammar and language rules when speaking or writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Acquire and use accurately a range of general academic and domain-specific words and phrases sufficient for reading, writing, speaking, and listening at the college and career readiness level; demonstrate independence in gathering vocabulary knowledge when encountering an unknown term important to comprehension or expression.
description_plain: Your child learns and uses a wide range of academic and subject-specific words for reading, writing, speaking, and listening, and can find out new word meanings on their own.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of figurative language, word relationships, and nuances in word meanings.
description_plain: Your child understands figurative language, how words relate to each other, and subtle differences in word meanings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine or clarify the meaning of unknown and multiple-meaning words and phrases by using context clues, analyzing meaningful word parts, and consulting general and specialized reference materials, as appropriate.
description_plain: Your child figures out the meaning of unknown or tricky words by using clues from the sentence, breaking the word into parts, or looking them up in dictionaries or other resources.

subject: Language Arts
description: Apply knowledge of language to understand how language functions in different contexts, to make effective choices for meaning or style, and to comprehend more fully when reading or listening.
description_plain: Your child uses what they know about language to understand how it works in different situations and to choose words and styles that fit what they want to say or understand better when reading or listening.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.
description_plain: Your child uses correct capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English grammar and usage when writing or speaking.
description_plain: Your child uses correct grammar and word usage when writing or speaking.

subject: Language Arts
description: Produce complete sentences when appropriate to task and situation in order to provide requested detail or clarification.
description_plain: Your child can speak in complete sentences to give details or explain when needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Create audio recordings of stories or poems; add drawings or other visual displays to stories or recounts of experiences when appropriate to clarify ideas, thoughts, and feelings.
description_plain: Your child can make audio recordings of stories or poems and add pictures or other visuals to help explain their ideas and feelings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Tell a story or recount an experience with appropriate facts and relevant, descriptive details, speaking audibly in coherent sentences.
description_plain: Your child can tell a story or share an experience using clear facts and details, speaking loudly and clearly in complete sentences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ask and answer questions about what a speaker says in order to clarify comprehension, gather additional information, or deepen understanding of a topic or issue.
description_plain: Your child can ask and answer questions about what someone says to better understand the topic or get more information.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recount or describe key ideas or details from a text read aloud or information presented orally or through other media.
description_plain: Your child can retell or describe important ideas or details from stories or information they hear.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ask for clarification and further explanation as needed about the topics and texts under discussion.
description_plain: Your child can ask for help or more explanation when they don’t understand something in a discussion.

subject: Language Arts
description: Build on others' talk in conversations by linking their comments to the remarks of others.
description_plain: Your child can add to conversations by connecting what they say to what others have said.

subject: Language Arts
description: Follow agreed-upon rules for discussions (e.g., gaining the floor in respectful ways, listening to others with care, speaking one at a time about the topics and texts under discussion).
description_plain: Your child can follow simple rules for talking with others, like waiting their turn and listening carefully.

subject: Language Arts
description: Participate in collaborative conversations with diverse partners about grade 2 topics and texts with peers and adults in small and larger groups.
description_plain: Your child can join in group talks with classmates and adults about grade 2 topics and stories.

subject: Language Arts
description: Adapt speech to a variety of contexts and communicative tasks, demonstrating command of formal English when indicated or appropriate.
description_plain: Your child changes how they speak depending on the situation and uses formal English when needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Make strategic use of digital media and visual displays of data to express information and enhance understanding of presentations.
description_plain: Your child uses digital tools and visuals to share information and make presentations easier to understand.

subject: Language Arts
description: Present information, findings, and supporting evidence such that listeners can follow the line of reasoning and the organization, development, and style are appropriate to task, purpose, and audience.
description_plain: Your child shares information and evidence clearly so listeners can follow their ideas and the way they organize their talk fits the purpose and audience.

subject: Language Arts
description: Evaluate a speaker's point of view, reasoning, and use of evidence and rhetoric.
description_plain: Your child thinks about a speaker’s opinion, reasons, and evidence to decide how strong their argument is.

subject: Language Arts
description: Integrate and evaluate information presented in diverse media and formats, including visually, quantitatively, and orally.
description_plain: Your child combines and evaluates information from different sources like pictures, numbers, and spoken words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Prepare for and participate effectively in a range of conversations and collaborations with diverse partners, building on others' ideas and expressing their own clearly and persuasively.
description_plain: Your child gets ready for and takes part in conversations with different people, building on others’ ideas and sharing their own clearly and confidently.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recall information from experiences or gather information from provided sources to answer a question.
description_plain: Your child can remember information from their own experiences or find facts from books or other sources to answer questions.

subject: Language Arts
description: Participate in shared research and writing projects (e.g., read a number of books on a single topic to produce a report; record science observations).
description_plain: Your child can work with others to research a topic by reading several books and then write a report or record observations.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support from adults, use a variety of digital tools to produce and publish writing, including in collaboration with peers.
description_plain: With help from adults, your child can use computers or other digital tools to write and share their work, sometimes working with friends.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support from adults and peers, focus on a topic and strengthen writing as needed by revising and editing.
description_plain: With support, your child can focus on a topic and improve their writing by making changes and fixing mistakes.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write narratives in which they recount a well-elaborated event or short sequence of events, include details to describe actions, thoughts, and feelings, use temporal words to signal event order, and provide a sense of closure.
description_plain: Your child can write stories that tell about an event with details about actions, thoughts, and feelings, use words that show order, and finish with a clear ending.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write informative/explanatory texts in which they introduce a topic, use facts and definitions to develop points, and provide a concluding statement or section.
description_plain: Your child can write informative texts that introduce a topic, give facts and explanations, and end with a conclusion.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write opinion pieces in which they introduce the topic or book they are writing about, state an opinion, supply reasons that support the opinion, use linking words (e.g., because, and, also) to connect opinion and reasons, and provide a concluding statement or section.
description_plain: Your child can write opinion pieces that state their opinion, give reasons to support it using connecting words, and finish with a closing statement.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write routinely over extended time frames (time for research, reflection, and revision) and shorter time frames (a single sitting or a day or two) for a range of tasks, purposes, and audiences.
description_plain: Your child can write regularly over short and long periods for different tasks and audiences, including time to research and revise.

subject: Language Arts
description: Draw evidence from literary or informational texts to support analysis, reflection, and research.
description_plain: Your child will find and use evidence from stories or information texts to support their thinking and research.

subject: Language Arts
description: Gather relevant information from multiple print and digital sources, assess the credibility and accuracy of each source, and integrate the information while avoiding plagiarism.
description_plain: Your child can gather information from books and online sources, check if the information is trustworthy, and use it without copying.

subject: Language Arts
description: Conduct short as well as more sustained research projects based on focused questions, demonstrating understanding of the subject under investigation.
description_plain: Your child will do short and longer research projects by asking questions and showing they understand the topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use technology, including the Internet, to produce and publish writing and to interact and collaborate with others.
description_plain: Your child can use technology like the Internet to write, publish, and work with others.

subject: Language Arts
description: Develop and strengthen writing as needed by planning, revising, editing, rewriting, or trying a new approach.
description_plain: Your child will improve their writing by planning, revising, editing, or trying new ways to express ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Produce clear and coherent writing in which the development, organization, and style are appropriate to task, purpose, and audience.
description_plain: Your child can write clearly and organized for the purpose and audience they are writing for.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write narratives to develop real or imagined experiences or events using effective technique, well-chosen details, and well-structured event sequences.
description_plain: Your child will write stories about real or imagined events using good details and clear order.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write informative/explanatory texts to examine and convey complex ideas and information clearly and accurately through the effective selection, organization, and analysis of content.
description_plain: Your child can write informative texts that explain complex ideas clearly by choosing and organizing information well.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write arguments to support claims in an analysis of substantive topics or texts, using valid reasoning and relevant and sufficient evidence.
description_plain: Your child will write arguments that support their claims with good reasons and enough evidence.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use context to confirm or self-correct word recognition and understanding, rereading as necessary.
description_plain: Your child can use clues from the sentence to figure out or fix words they don’t know, rereading if needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read on-level text orally with accuracy, appropriate rate, and expression on successive readings.
description_plain: Your child can read grade-level texts out loud with accuracy, proper speed, and expression after practicing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read on-level text with purpose and understanding.
description_plain: Your child can read grade-level texts with a clear purpose and understanding.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read with sufficient accuracy and fluency to support comprehension.
description_plain: Your child can read accurately and smoothly enough to understand what they read.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recognize and read grade-appropriate irregularly spelled words.
description_plain: Your child can recognize and read common words that don’t follow usual spelling rules.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify words with inconsistent but common spelling-sound correspondences.
description_plain: Your child can identify words that have common but tricky spelling and sound patterns.

subject: Language Arts
description: Decode words with common prefixes and suffixes.
description_plain: Your child can read words with common beginnings and endings like prefixes and suffixes.

subject: Language Arts
description: Decode regularly spelled two-syllable words with long vowels.
description_plain: Your child can read two-syllable words that have long vowel sounds and regular spelling.

subject: Language Arts
description: Know spelling-sound correspondences for additional common vowel teams.
description_plain: Your child knows the sounds made by common vowel teams when reading.

subject: Language Arts
description: Distinguish long and short vowels when reading regularly spelled one-syllable words.
description_plain: Your child can tell the difference between long and short vowel sounds in one-syllable words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Know and apply grade-level phonics and word analysis skills in decoding words.
description_plain: Your child can use phonics and word skills to sound out and read words at grade level.

subject: Language Arts
description: By the end of year, read and comprehend informational texts, including history/social studies, science, and technical texts, in the grades 2—3 text complexity band proficiently, with scaffolding as needed at the high end of the range.
description_plain: By the end of the year, your child can read and understand grade 2-3 informational texts about history, science, and technology, with some help if needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast the most important points presented by two texts on the same topic.
description_plain: Your child can compare the most important ideas from two texts about the same topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe how reasons support specific points the author makes in a text.
description_plain: Your child can explain how reasons in a text support the author’s main points.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain how specific images (e.g., a diagram showing how a machine works) contribute to and clarify a text.
description_plain: Your child can explain how pictures or diagrams help explain or clarify the information in a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify the main purpose of a text, including what the author wants to answer, explain, or describe.
description_plain: Your child can identify the main purpose of a text, like what the author wants to explain or describe.

subject: Language Arts
description: Know and use various text features (e.g., captions, bold print, subheadings, glossaries, indexes, electronic menus, icons) to locate key facts or information in a text efficiently.
description_plain: Your child can use parts of a text like captions, bold words, and headings to quickly find important facts or information.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the meaning of words and phrases in a text relevant to a grade 2 topic or subject area.
description_plain: Your child can figure out the meaning of words and phrases related to grade 2 topics when reading.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe the connection between a series of historical events, scientific ideas or concepts, or steps in technical procedures in a text.
description_plain: Your child can describe how a series of events, ideas, or steps are connected in a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify the main topic of a multiparagraph text as well as the focus of specific paragraphs within the text.
description_plain: Your child can tell the main topic of a text with several paragraphs and what each paragraph focuses on.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ask and answer such questions as who, what, where, when, why, and how to demonstrate understanding of key details in a text.
description_plain: Your child can ask and answer questions like who, what, where, when, why, and how to show they understand important details in a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: By the end of the year, read and comprehend literature, including stories and poetry, in the grades 2—3 text complexity band proficiently, with scaffolding as needed at the high end of the range.
description_plain: By the end of the year, your child can read and understand grade 2-3 stories and poems, with some help if needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast two or more versions of the same story (e.g., Cinderella stories) by different authors or from different cultures.
description_plain: Your child can compare and talk about differences and similarities between two or more versions of the same story by different authors or cultures.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use information gained from the illustrations and words in a print or digital text to demonstrate understanding of its characters, setting, or plot.
description_plain: Your child can use pictures and words in a book or digital story to show they understand the characters, setting, or plot.

subject: Language Arts
description: Acknowledge differences in the points of view of characters, including by speaking in a different voice for each character when reading dialogue aloud.
description_plain: Your child can notice and talk about different points of view of characters, even using different voices when reading their dialogue aloud.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe the overall structure of a story, including describing how the beginning introduces the story and the ending concludes the action.
description_plain: Your child can describe how a story is organized, including how the beginning starts the story and the ending wraps it up.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe how words and phrases (e.g., regular beats, alliteration, rhymes, repeated lines) supply rhythm and meaning in a story, poem, or song.
description_plain: Your child can explain how words and phrases like rhymes and repeated lines create rhythm and add meaning in stories, poems, or songs.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe how characters in a story respond to major events and challenges.
description_plain: Your child can describe how characters in a story react to important events and problems.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recount stories, including fables and folktales from diverse cultures, and determine their central message, lesson, or moral.
description_plain: Your child can retell stories from different cultures and explain the main message or lesson.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ask and answer such questions as who, what, where, when, why, and how to demonstrate understanding of key details in a text.
description_plain: Your child can ask and answer questions like who, what, where, when, why, and how to show they understand important details in a story.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read and comprehend complex literary and informational texts independently and proficiently.
description_plain: Your child can read and understand difficult stories and information on their own.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how two or more texts address similar themes or topics in order to build knowledge or to compare the approaches the authors take.
description_plain: Your child can compare how two or more texts talk about the same ideas or topics and explain the different ways the authors write about them.

subject: Language Arts
description: Delineate and evaluate the argument and specific claims in a text, including the validity of the reasoning as well as the relevance and sufficiency of the evidence.
description_plain: Your child can find and think about the main points and reasons in a text and decide if the evidence is strong and relevant.

subject: Language Arts
description: Integrate and evaluate content presented in diverse media and formats, including visually and quantitatively, as well as in words.
description_plain: Your child can understand and judge information presented in different ways, like pictures, numbers, and words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Assess how point of view or purpose shapes the content and style of a text.
description_plain: Your child can explain how the author's point of view or purpose affects what is written and how it is written.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze the structure of texts, including how specific sentences, paragraphs, and larger portions of the text (e.g., a section, chapter, scene, or stanza) relate to each other and the whole.
description_plain: Your child can look at how sentences and paragraphs fit together in a text and explain how they help the whole story or article.

subject: Language Arts
description: Interpret words and phrases as they are used in a text, including determining technical, connotative, and figurative meanings, and analyze how specific word choices shape meaning or tone.
description_plain: Your child can figure out the meaning of words and phrases in a text, including special or figurative meanings, and explain how word choices affect the feeling or meaning.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how and why individuals, events, and ideas develop and interact over the course of a text.
description_plain: Your child can explain how people, events, and ideas change and connect throughout a story or text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine central ideas or themes of a text and analyze their development; summarize the key supporting details and ideas.
description_plain: Your child can find the main ideas or themes in a text, explain how they develop, and summarize the important details.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read closely to determine what the text says explicitly and to make logical inferences from it; cite specific textual evidence when writing or speaking to support conclusions drawn from the text.
description_plain: Your child can read carefully to understand what a text says and make logical guesses, using examples from the text to support their ideas.

subject: Math
description: Partition circles and rectangles into two, three, or four equal shares, describe the shares using the words halves, thirds, half of, a third of, etc., and describe the whole as two halves, three thirds, four fourths. Recognize that equal shares of identical wholes need not have the same shape.
description_plain: Your child can divide circles and rectangles into 2, 3, or 4 equal parts, name these parts as halves, thirds, or fourths, and understand that equal parts can look different but still be the same size.

subject: Math
description: Partition a rectangle into rows and columns of same-size squares and count to find the total number of them.
description_plain: Your child can divide a rectangle into equal rows and columns of squares and count how many squares there are in total.

subject: Math
description: Recognize and draw shapes having specified attributes, such as a given number of angles or a given number of equal faces. Identify triangles, quadrilaterals, pentagons, hexagons, and cubes.
description_plain: Your child can recognize and draw shapes with certain features, like a specific number of angles or equal sides, and can identify shapes like triangles, quadrilaterals, pentagons, hexagons, and cubes.

subject: Math
description: Reason with shapes and their attributes.
description_plain: Your child understands how to think about shapes and their features.

subject: Math
description: Draw a picture graph and a bar graph (with single-unit scale) to represent a data set with up to four categories. Solve simple put-together, take-apart, and compare problems using information presented in a bar graph.
description_plain: Your child can make picture and bar graphs with up to four groups and use them to solve simple problems about adding, taking away, or comparing amounts.

subject: Math
description: Generate measurement data by measuring lengths of several objects to the nearest whole unit, or by making repeated measurements of the same object. Show the measurements by making a line plot, where the horizontal scale is marked off in whole-number units.
description_plain: Your child can measure the length of several objects or the same object many times, then show these measurements on a line plot using whole numbers.

subject: Math
description: Solve word problems involving dollar bills, quarters, dimes, nickels, and pennies, using $ and ¢ symbols appropriately.
description_plain: Your child can solve money word problems using dollar bills and coins, and write amounts with $ and ¢ symbols correctly.

subject: Math
description: Tell and write time from analog and digital clocks to the nearest five minutes, using a.m. and p.m.
description_plain: Your child can tell and write time to the nearest five minutes on both analog and digital clocks, using a.m. and p.m.

subject: Math
description: Represent whole numbers as lengths from 0 on a number line diagram with equally spaced points corresponding to the numbers 0, 1, 2, …, and represent whole-number sums and differences within 100 on a number line diagram.
description_plain: Your child can show whole numbers on a number line and use the line to add and subtract numbers up to 100.

subject: Math
description: Use addition and subtraction within 100 to solve word problems involving lengths that are given in the same units, e.g., by using drawings (such as drawings of rulers) and equations with a symbol for the unknown number to represent the problem.
description_plain: Your child can solve word problems about lengths using addition and subtraction within 100, with drawings or equations that include an unknown number.

subject: Math
description: Relate addition and subtraction to length.
description_plain: Your child understands how addition and subtraction relate to measuring length.

subject: Math
description: Measure to determine how much longer one object is than another, expressing the length difference in terms of a standard length unit.
description_plain: Your child can measure how much longer one object is than another and say the difference using standard units like inches or centimeters.

subject: Math
description: Estimate lengths using units of inches, feet, centimeters, and meters.
description_plain: Your child can guess the length of objects using units like inches, feet, centimeters, or meters.

subject: Math
description: Measure the length of an object twice, using length units of different lengths for the two measurements; describe how the two measurements relate to the size of the unit chosen.
description_plain: Your child can measure the same object twice using different units and explain how the measurements change based on the unit size.

subject: Math
description: Measure the length of an object by selecting and using appropriate tools such as rulers, yardsticks, meter sticks, and measuring tapes.
description_plain: Your child can choose and use tools like rulers or measuring tapes to measure how long things are.

subject: Math
description: Measure and estimate lengths in standard units.
description_plain: Your child can measure and estimate lengths using standard units.

subject: Math
description: Explain why addition and subtraction strategies work, using place value and the properties of operations.
description_plain: Your child can explain why their ways of adding and subtracting work by using place value and math rules.

subject: Math
description: Mentally add 10 or 100 to a given number 100—900, and mentally subtract 10 or 100 from a given number 100—900.
description_plain: Your child can quickly add or subtract 10 or 100 to numbers between 100 and 900 in their head.

subject: Math
description: Add and subtract within 1000, using concrete models or drawings and strategies based on place value, properties of operations, and/or the relationship between addition and subtraction; relate the strategy to a written method. Understand that in adding or subtracting three-digit numbers, one adds or subtracts hundreds and hundreds, tens and tens, ones and ones; and sometimes it is necessary to compose or decompose tens or hundreds.
description_plain: Your child can add and subtract numbers up to 1000 using drawings or objects and understand how to add or subtract hundreds, tens, and ones.

subject: Math
description: Add up to four two-digit numbers using strategies based on place value and properties of operations.
description_plain: Your child can add up to four two-digit numbers using place value and math rules.

subject: Math
description: Fluently add and subtract within 100 using strategies based on place value, properties of operations, and/or the relationship between addition and subtraction.
description_plain: Your child can quickly add and subtract numbers within 100 using place value and math rules.

subject: Math
description: Use place value understanding and properties of operations to add and subtract.
description_plain: Your child uses place value and math rules to add and subtract numbers.

subject: Math
description: Compare two three-digit numbers based on meanings of the hundreds, tens, and ones digits, using >, =, and < symbols to record the results of comparisons.
description_plain: Your child can compare two three-digit numbers by looking at the hundreds, tens, and ones digits and use >, =, or < to show which is bigger or if they are equal.

subject: Math
description: Read and write numbers to 1000 using base-ten numerals, number names, and expanded form.
description_plain: Your child can read and write numbers up to 1000 in regular form, word form, and expanded form.

subject: Math
description: Count within 1000; skip-count by 5s, 10s, and 100s.
description_plain: Your child can count numbers up to 1000 and skip-count by 5s, 10s, and 100s.

subject: Math
description: The numbers 100, 200, 300, 400, 500, 600, 700, 800, 900 refer to one, two, three, four, five, six, seven, eight, or nine hundreds (and 0 tens and 0 ones).
description_plain: Your child understands that numbers like 100, 200, 300, and so on mean one, two, three, etc., hundreds with zero tens and ones.

subject: Math
description: 100 can be thought of as a bundle of ten tens — called a "hundred."
description_plain: Your child knows that 100 is the same as a bundle of ten tens, called a 'hundred.'

subject: Math
description: Understand that the three digits of a three-digit number represent amounts of hundreds, tens, and ones; e.g., 706 equals 7 hundreds, 0 tens, and 6 ones. Understand the following as special cases:
description_plain: Your child understands that a three-digit number shows how many hundreds, tens, and ones it has, like 706 means 7 hundreds, 0 tens, and 6 ones.

subject: Math
description: Use addition to find the total number of objects arranged in rectangular arrays with up to 5 rows and up to 5 columns; write an equation to express the total as a sum of equal addends.
description_plain: Your child can use addition to find the total number of objects arranged in rows and columns (up to 5 rows and 5 columns) and write an equation showing the total as equal groups added together.

subject: Math
description: Determine whether a group of objects (up to 20) has an odd or even number of members, e.g., by pairing objects or counting them by 2s; write an equation to express an even number as a sum of two equal addends.
description_plain: Your child can tell if a group of up to 20 objects has an odd or even number by pairing them or counting by twos, and write an equation showing an even number as two equal groups added together.

subject: Math
description: Work with equal groups of objects to gain foundations for multiplication.
description_plain: Your child works with equal groups of objects to start learning multiplication.

subject: Math
description: Fluently add and subtract within 20 using mental strategies. By end of Grade 2, know from memory all sums of two one-digit numbers.
description_plain: Your child can quickly add and subtract numbers within 20 in their head and knows all sums of two one-digit numbers by memory.

subject: Math
description: Use addition and subtraction within 100 to solve one- and two-step word problems involving situations of adding to, taking from, putting together, taking apart, and comparing, with unknowns in all positions, e.g., by using drawings and equations with a symbol for the unknown number to represent the problem.
description_plain: Your child can solve one- and two-step word problems using addition and subtraction within 100, with unknown numbers anywhere in the problem, using drawings or equations.

subject: Math
description: Represent and solve problems involving addition and subtraction.
description_plain: Your child can show and solve problems that use addition and subtraction.

subject: Math
description: Look for and express regularity in repeated reasoning.
description_plain: Your child can notice and explain patterns when solving problems repeatedly.

subject: Math
description: Look for and make use of structure.
description_plain: Your child can find and use the way things are organized or structured to help solve problems.

subject: Math
description: Use appropriate tools strategically.
description_plain: Your child can choose and use the right tools to help solve math problems.

subject: Math
description: Construct viable arguments and critique the reasoning of others.
description_plain: Your child can explain their thinking clearly and listen to others' ideas to decide if they make sense.

subject: Math
description: Reason abstractly and quantitatively.
description_plain: Your child can think about math problems both in general ideas and with real numbers.

subject: Math
description: Make sense of problems and persevere in solving them.
description_plain: Your child can understand problems and keep trying until they find a solution.

subject: Math
description: Relating the choice of measures of center and variability to the shape of the data distribution and the context in which the data were gathered.
description_plain: Your child can choose the best way to describe data based on its shape and the situation it came from.

subject: Math
description: Giving quantitative measures of center (median and/or mean) and variability (interquartile range and/or mean absolute deviation), as well as describing any overall pattern and any striking deviations from the overall pattern with reference to the context in which the data were gathered.
description_plain: Your child can find and explain numbers that show the center and spread of data and describe patterns or unusual points.

subject: Math
description: Describing the nature of the attribute under investigation, including how it was measured and its units of measurement.
description_plain: Your child can describe what is being measured in data, how it was measured, and the units used.

subject: Math
description: Reporting the number of observations.
description_plain: Your child can tell how many data points were collected.

subject: Math
description: Summarize numerical data sets in relation to their context, such as by:
description_plain: Your child can summarize sets of numbers by explaining what they mean in their real-life context.

subject: Math
description: Display numerical data in plots on a number line, including dot plots, histograms, and box plots.
description_plain: Your child can show number data on number lines using dot plots, histograms, and box plots.

subject: Math
description: Summarize and describe distributions.
description_plain: Your child can summarize and describe how data is spread out and shaped.

subject: Math
description: Recognize that a measure of center for a numerical data set summarizes all of its values with a single number, while a measure of variation describes how its values vary with a single number.
description_plain: Your child understands that one number can show the center of data and another number can show how much the data varies.

subject: Math
description: Understand that a set of data collected to answer a statistical question has a distribution which can be described by its center, spread, and overall shape.
description_plain: Your child understands that data collected to answer a question has a pattern that can be described by its center, spread, and shape.

subject: Math
description: Recognize a statistical question as one that anticipates variability in the data related to the question and accounts for it in the answers.
description_plain: Your child knows a statistical question expects different answers because the data can vary.

subject: Math
description: Develop understanding of statistical variability.
description_plain: Your child learns about how data can vary and what that means.

subject: Math
description: Use variables to represent two quantities in a real-world problem that change in relationship to one another; write an equation to express one quantity, thought of as the dependent variable, in terms of the other quantity, thought of as the independent variable. Analyze the relationship between the dependent and independent variables using graphs and tables, and relate these to the equation.
description_plain: Your child can use letters to show how two changing amounts are related, write equations for these, and understand the relationship by looking at graphs and tables.

subject: Math
description: Represent and analyze quantitative relationships between dependent and independent variables.
description_plain: Your child can show and study how one number changes when another number changes.

subject: Math
description: Write an inequality of the form x > c or x < c to represent a constraint or condition in a real-world or mathematical problem. Recognize that inequalities of the form x > c or x < c have infinitely many solutions; represent solutions of such inequalities on number line diagrams.
description_plain: Your child can write inequalities like x > c or x < c to show limits in problems and draw their solutions on a number line.

subject: Math
description: Solve real-world and mathematical problems by writing and solving equations of the form x + p = q and px = q for cases in which p, q and x are all nonnegative rational numbers.
description_plain: Your child can solve real-life and math problems by writing and solving simple equations like x + p = q or px = q with positive numbers.

subject: Math
description: Use variables to represent numbers and write expressions when solving a real-world or mathematical problem; understand that a variable can represent an unknown number, or, depending on the purpose at hand, any number in a specified set.
description_plain: Your child can use letters to stand for numbers and write expressions to solve problems, understanding that letters can mean unknown or any number in a group.

subject: Math
description: Understand solving an equation or inequality as a process of answering a question: which values from a specified set, if any, make the equation or inequality true? Use substitution to determine whether a given number in a specified set makes an equation or inequality true.
description_plain: Your child understands that solving an equation or inequality means finding which numbers make it true and can check this by plugging in numbers.

subject: Math
description: Reason about and solve one-variable equations and inequalities.
description_plain: Your child can think through and solve equations and inequalities that have one variable.

subject: Math
description: Identify when two expressions are equivalent (i.e., when the two expressions name the same number regardless of which value is substituted into them).
description_plain: Your child can tell when two expressions are equal because they give the same result no matter what number is used.

subject: Math
description: Apply the properties of operations to generate equivalent expressions.
description_plain: Your child can use math rules to create expressions that mean the same thing.

subject: Math
description: Evaluate expressions at specific values of their variables. Include expressions that arise from formulas used in real-world problems. Perform arithmetic operations, including those involving whole-number exponents, in the conventional order when there are no parentheses to specify a particular order (Order of Operations).
description_plain: Your child can find the value of expressions when numbers are plugged in, including using exponents and following the correct order of operations.

subject: Math
description: Identify parts of an expression using mathematical terms (sum, term, product, factor, quotient, coefficient); view one or more parts of an expression as a single entity.
description_plain: Your child can name parts of an expression like sum, product, factor, and coefficient, and see parts of an expression as one piece.

subject: Math
description: Write expressions that record operations with numbers and with letters standing for numbers.
description_plain: Your child can write expressions that show math operations using numbers and letters for numbers.

subject: Math
description: Write, read, and evaluate expressions in which letters stand for numbers.
description_plain: Your child can write, read, and find the value of expressions where letters stand for numbers.

subject: Math
description: Write and evaluate numerical expressions involving whole-number exponents.
description_plain: Your child can write and calculate expressions that include whole-number exponents.

subject: Math
description: Apply and extend previous understandings of arithmetic to algebraic expressions.
description_plain: Your child can use what they know about arithmetic to work with expressions that include variables.

subject: Math
description: Solve real-world and mathematical problems by graphing points in all four quadrants of the coordinate plane. Include use of coordinates and absolute value to find distances between points with the same first coordinate or the same second coordinate.
description_plain: Your child can solve problems by plotting points in all four parts of the coordinate plane and use coordinates and absolute value to find distances between points.

subject: Math
description: Distinguish comparisons of absolute value from statements about order.
description_plain: Your child can tell the difference between comparing absolute values and comparing which number is bigger or smaller.

subject: Math
description: Understand the absolute value of a rational number as its distance from 0 on the number line; interpret absolute value as magnitude for a positive or negative quantity in a real-world situation.
description_plain: Your child understands absolute value as how far a number is from zero on the number line and can explain this in real-life situations.

subject: Math
description: Write, interpret, and explain statements of order for rational numbers in real-world contexts.
description_plain: Your child can write and explain order statements about rational numbers in real-world examples.

subject: Math
description: Interpret statements of inequality as statements about the relative position of two numbers on a number line diagram.
description_plain: Your child can understand inequalities as showing where numbers are positioned compared to each other on a number line.

subject: Math
description: Understand ordering and absolute value of rational numbers.
description_plain: Your child understands how to order rational numbers and knows what absolute value means.

subject: Math
description: Find and position integers and other rational numbers on a horizontal or vertical number line diagram; find and position pairs of integers and other rational numbers on a coordinate plane.
description_plain: Your child can find and place integers and rational numbers on number lines and on coordinate planes.

subject: Math
description: Understand signs of numbers in ordered pairs as indicating locations in quadrants of the coordinate plane; recognize that when two ordered pairs differ only by signs, the locations of the points are related by reflections across one or both axes.
description_plain: Your child understands that the signs in ordered pairs show where points are on the coordinate plane and can see how points reflect across axes.

subject: Math
description: Recognize opposite signs of numbers as indicating locations on opposite sides of 0 on the number line; recognize that the opposite of the opposite of a number is the number itself, e.g., -(-3) = 3, and that 0 is its own opposite.
description_plain: Your child knows that opposite signs mean numbers are on opposite sides of zero and that the opposite of the opposite number is the original number.

subject: Math
description: Understand a rational number as a point on the number line. Extend number line diagrams and coordinate axes familiar from previous grades to represent points on the line and in the plane with negative number coordinates.
description_plain: Your child understands that rational numbers can be shown as points on number lines and coordinate planes, including negative numbers.

subject: Math
description: Understand that positive and negative numbers are used together to describe quantities having opposite directions or values (e.g., temperature above/below zero, elevation above/below sea level, credits/debits, positive/negative electric charge); use positive and negative numbers to represent quantities in real-world contexts, explaining the meaning of 0 in each situation.
description_plain: Your child knows that positive and negative numbers describe things going in opposite directions or having opposite values and can explain what zero means in these situations.

subject: Math
description: Apply and extend previous understandings of numbers to the system of rational numbers.
description_plain: Your child can use what they know about numbers to understand and work with rational numbers.

subject: Math
description: Find the greatest common factor of two whole numbers less than or equal to 100 and the least common multiple of two whole numbers less than or equal to 12. Use the distributive property to express a sum of two whole numbers 1—100 with a common factor as a multiple of a sum of two whole numbers with no common factor.
description_plain: Your child can find the greatest common factor of two numbers up to 100 and the least common multiple of two numbers up to 12, and use these to rewrite sums in simpler forms.

subject: Math
description: Fluently add, subtract, multiply, and divide multi-digit decimals using the standard algorithm for each operation.
description_plain: Your child can quickly add, subtract, multiply, and divide decimals with many digits using standard methods.

subject: Math
description: Fluently divide multi-digit numbers using the standard algorithm.
description_plain: Your child can quickly divide large numbers using the usual steps.

subject: Math
description: Compute fluently with multi-digit numbers and find common factors and multiples.
description_plain: Your child can work quickly with large numbers and find common factors and multiples.

subject: Math
description: Interpret and compute quotients of fractions, and solve word problems involving division of fractions by fractions, e.g., by using visual fraction models and equations to represent the problem.
description_plain: Your child can understand and calculate division of fractions by fractions and solve related word problems using pictures and equations.

subject: Math
description: Apply and extend previous understandings of multiplication and division to divide fractions by fractions.
description_plain: Your child can use what they know about multiplication and division to divide fractions by fractions.

subject: Math
description: Use ratio reasoning to convert measurement units; manipulate and transform units appropriately when multiplying or dividing quantities.
description_plain: Your child can use ratio thinking to change measurement units and correctly adjust units when multiplying or dividing.

subject: Math
description: Find a percent of a quantity as a rate per 100 (e.g., 30% of a quantity means 30/100 times the quantity); solve problems involving finding the whole, given a part and the percent.
description_plain: Your child can find a percent of a number by thinking of it as parts per 100 and solve problems where they find the whole from a part and a percent.

subject: Math
description: Solve unit rate problems including those involving unit pricing and constant speed.
description_plain: Your child can solve problems about unit rates, like figuring out prices per item or speeds.

subject: Math
description: Make tables of equivalent ratios relating quantities with whole number measurements, find missing values in the tables, and plot the pairs of values on the coordinate plane. Use tables to compare ratios.
description_plain: Your child can make tables of equivalent ratios with whole numbers, find missing numbers, plot these on graphs, and use tables to compare ratios.

subject: Math
description: Use ratio and rate reasoning to solve real-world and mathematical problems, e.g., by reasoning about tables of equivalent ratios, tape diagrams, double number line diagrams, or equations.
description_plain: Your child can use ratio and rate thinking to solve real-life and math problems using tables, diagrams, or equations.

subject: Math
description: Understand the concept of a unit rate a/b associated with a ratio a:b with b ≠ 0, and use rate language in the context of a ratio relationship.
description_plain: Your child understands what a unit rate is and can use words to describe rates in ratio relationships.

subject: Math
description: Understand the concept of a ratio and use ratio language to describe a ratio relationship between two quantities.
description_plain: Your child understands what a ratio is and can use words to explain the relationship between two amounts.

subject: Math
description: Understand ratio concepts and use ratio reasoning to solve problems.
description_plain: Your child understands ratios and can use ratio thinking to solve problems.

subject: Math
description: Represent three-dimensional figures using nets made up of rectangles and triangles, and use the nets to find the surface area of these figures. Apply these techniques in the context of solving real-world and mathematical problems.
description_plain: Your child can draw flat patterns made of rectangles and triangles that fold into 3D shapes, and use these patterns to find the total surface area of the shapes, solving real-life and math problems.

subject: Math
description: Draw polygons in the coordinate plane given coordinates for the vertices; use coordinates to find the length of a side joining points with the same first coordinate or the same second coordinate. Apply these techniques in the context of solving real-world and mathematical problems.
description_plain: Your child can plot shapes on a grid using points and find the length of sides when points share the same x or y coordinate, applying this to solve real-world and math problems.

subject: Math
description: Find the volume of a right rectangular prism with fractional edge lengths by packing it with unit cubes of the appropriate unit fraction edge lengths, and show that the volume is the same as would be found by multiplying the edge lengths of the prism. Apply the formulas V = l w h and V = b h to find volumes of right rectangular prisms with fractional edge lengths in the context of solving real-world and mathematical problems.
description_plain: Your child can find the volume of a box-shaped figure with fractional side lengths by filling it with smaller cubes and by multiplying the side lengths, using formulas to solve real-world and math problems.

subject: Math
description: Find the area of right triangles, other triangles, special quadrilaterals, and polygons by composing into rectangles or decomposing into triangles and other shapes; apply these techniques in the context of solving real-world and mathematical problems.
description_plain: Your child can find the area of right triangles, other triangles, special four-sided shapes, and polygons by breaking them into rectangles or triangles, and use these methods to solve real-world and math problems.

subject: Math
description: Solve real-world and mathematical problems involving area, surface area, and volume.
description_plain: Your child can solve problems that involve finding area, surface area, and volume in real-life and math situations.

subject: Math
description: Look for and express regularity in repeated reasoning.
description_plain: Your child can notice and explain patterns when solving problems repeatedly.

subject: Math
description: Look for and make use of structure.
description_plain: Your child can find and use the way things are organized or structured to help solve problems.

subject: Math
description: Use appropriate tools strategically.
description_plain: Your child can choose and use the right tools to help solve math problems.

subject: Math
description: Construct viable arguments and critique the reasoning of others.
description_plain: Your child can explain their thinking clearly and listen to others' ideas to decide if they make sense.

subject: Math
description: Reason abstractly and quantitatively.
description_plain: Your child can think about math problems both in general ideas and with real numbers.

subject: Math
description: Make sense of problems and persevere in solving them.
description_plain: Your child can understand problems and keep trying until they find a solution.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use words and phrases acquired through conversations, reading and being read to, and responding to texts.
description_plain: Your child can use new words and phrases learned from talking, reading, and listening to stories.

subject: Language Arts
description: Distinguish shades of meaning among verbs describing the same general action (e.g., walk, march, strut, prance) by acting out the meanings.
description_plain: Your child can tell the difference between similar action words (like walk, march, strut) by acting them out.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify real-life connections between words and their use (e.g., note places at school that are colorful).
description_plain: Your child can connect words to real-life places or things, like noticing colorful spots at school.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of frequently occurring verbs and adjectives by relating them to their opposites (antonyms).
description_plain: Your child can understand common verbs and adjectives by knowing their opposites.

subject: Language Arts
description: Sort common objects into categories (e.g., shapes, foods) to gain a sense of the concepts the categories represent.
description_plain: Your child can group common objects, like shapes or foods, to understand what the groups mean.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support from adults, explore word relationships and nuances in word meanings.
description_plain: With help from adults, your child explores how words are related and the different meanings they can have.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use the most frequently occurring inflections and affixes (e.g., -ed, -s, re-, un-, pre-, -ful, -less) as a clue to the meaning of an unknown word.
description_plain: Your child can use common word parts like -ed or re- to help figure out the meaning of new words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify new meanings for familiar words and apply them accurately (e.g., knowing duck is a bird and learning the verb to duck).
description_plain: Your child can learn new meanings for familiar words and use them correctly, like knowing 'duck' as a bird and also as a verb.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine or clarify the meaning of unknown and multiple-meaning words and phrases based on kindergarten reading and content.
description_plain: Your child can figure out the meaning of new or tricky words based on what they read and learn in kindergarten.

subject: Language Arts
description: Spell simple words phonetically, drawing on knowledge of sound-letter relationships.
description_plain: Your child can spell simple words by sounding them out using what they know about letters and sounds.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write a letter or letters for most consonant and short-vowel sounds (phonemes).
description_plain: Your child can write letters for most consonant and short vowel sounds.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recognize and name end punctuation.
description_plain: Your child can recognize and name punctuation marks at the end of sentences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Capitalize the first word in a sentence and the pronoun I.
description_plain: Your child can capitalize the first word in a sentence and the pronoun I.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.
description_plain: Your child can use correct capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Produce and expand complete sentences in shared language activities.
description_plain: Your child can make and add to complete sentences during shared speaking activities.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use the most frequently occurring prepositions (e.g., to, from, in, out, on, off, for, of, by, with).
description_plain: Your child can use common prepositions like to, from, in, and on.

subject: Language Arts
description: Understand and use question words (interrogatives) (e.g., who, what, where, when, why, how).
description_plain: Your child can understand and use question words like who, what, where, when, why, and how.

subject: Language Arts
description: Form regular plural nouns orally by adding /s/ or /es/ (e.g., dog, dogs; wish, wishes).
description_plain: Your child can say plural nouns by adding -s or -es, like dogs or wishes.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use frequently occurring nouns and verbs.
description_plain: Your child can use common nouns and verbs.

subject: Language Arts
description: Print many upper- and lowercase letters.
description_plain: Your child can print many uppercase and lowercase letters.

subject: Language Arts
